title: Floats and shortlist rows render in the candidate "Applications" panel as phantom jobs with live Move/Reject/Place actions
userImpact: On the candidate detail page (the demo screen), every float the recruiter recorded shows up as a ghost 'Untitled job' card stuck at stage 'Applied', and shortlist entries appear as 'Applied' jobs mixed in with real pipeline applications. Each ghost card exposes a working actions menu: 'Move to → Placed' (opens the placement-capture modal), 'Remove from job', and 'Reject…'. The recruiter sees duplicate/ghost data and can fire a real placement or rejection on a speculative float that has no pipeline meaning, undermining trust in front of the client.
fix: Add `.eq('application_type', 'standard')` to listApplicationsForCandidate in src/lib/db/applications.ts (~line 90), mirroring listApplicationsForJob (line 131) and listAllApplicationsByStage (line 225), which already carry this invariant. Floats have their own /candidates/[id]/floats tab and shortlists have /jobs/[id]/shortlist, so excluding them loses no information.

title: Candidate email stored verbatim on create/edit but every dedup lookup compares lowercased — creates duplicate candidates and breaks Outlook/apply-form matching
userImpact: A candidate added via the New/Edit Candidate form as 'John.Doe@Acme.com' is stored verbatim (Zod only .trim()s, column is plain text with no lowercasing trigger), but getCandidateByEmailForOrg, findCandidateByEmail and getCandidateByEmailLowercase all .eq against a lowercased value. So when the same person self-applies (apply form lowercases) a SECOND candidate row is created, Outlook email-sync fails to attach to the manual record, and LinkedIn email-fallback dedup misses. Classic 'why are there two of this guy?' the client will spot in a demo.
fix: Lowercase email at the write boundary so it matches every read path: in createCandidate (~line 358) and updateCandidate (~line 455) of src/lib/db/candidates.ts, write `email: input.email ? input.email.toLowerCase().trim() : null`. LinkedIn upsert already does this (candidates-linkedin.ts:216). Centralise in a small helper so all four write/read sites agree.

title: Raw Supabase/Postgres error objects logged to Sentry leak candidate PII (LinkedIn URL, email, name) — beforeSend scrub never touches serialized error details/exception values
userImpact: The Sentry beforeSend only redacts a fixed PII_KEYS list in event.extra/contexts; it never scrubs the serialized error object's `details` field (nor exception values). Candidate DB helpers (candidates.ts:385/424/464/699, candidate-cvs.ts:417, candidates-linkedin.ts:184/237) pass raw PostgREST error objects to Sentry.captureException. On a 23505 (e.g. the LinkedIn source_detail unique index), Postgres' DETAIL — `Key (organization_id, source_detail)=(<uuid>, https://www.linkedin.com/in/jane-doe) already exists.` — lands in event.extra.__serialized__.details unredacted. For a GDPR-bound UK agency this is a compliance breach and undermines the 'audit-ready, PII-safe' pitch. The author already applied the safe wrap in parse-cv.ts but not in the candidate helpers.
fix: Either (preferred) stop passing raw Supabase error objects to Sentry from the candidate DB helpers — wrap as `new Error(error.code ?? 'db_error')` with PII-free tags, mirroring parse-cv.ts:103-105; and/or extend beforeSend in sentry.server.config.ts to walk event.exception.values[].value and strip Postgres `Key (...)=(...)` DETAIL fragments and email/URL substrings from serialized error fields.

title: CV upload and 'Accept all' succeed but the page doesn't update until manual refresh (revalidatePath without router.refresh)
userImpact: Two instances of the same root cause: a server action calls revalidatePath() but the client handler never calls router.refresh(), so the mounted RSC tree doesn't re-render. (1) After CV upload, the recruiter sees a 'CV uploaded — parsing…' toast and the file picker clears, but no parsing spinner / review panel appears — it looks like the upload was lost (cv-upload.tsx:30). (2) After clicking 'Accept all', a toast says 'Filled N fields from the CV' and the sheet closes, but the Employment/Skills/Location panels still show '—' — it looks like the accept silently did nothing (cv-review-panel.tsx CompleteState ~line 190). Both contradict an explicit success toast on the flagship AI-extraction flow during the demo.
fix: Add `const router = useRouter()` and call router.refresh() after the success path in BOTH: cv-upload.tsx handleSubmit (after toast.success/reset) and cv-review-panel.tsx CompleteState.onAcceptAll (after setOpen(false)). This matches the existing convention in log-activity-form.tsx:63, candidate-applications.tsx:129/274, float-row-actions.tsx:40 and float-note-editor.tsx:45.

title: CV upload returns ok:true even when the Inngest parse dispatch failed — shows a 'parsing…' success toast over a 'parsing failed' panel
userImpact: When inngest.send('cv/uploaded') throws, uploadCVAction flips the row to parsing_status='failed' but still returns { ok: true }, so cv-upload.tsx fires the green 'CV uploaded — parsing…' toast while the panel simultaneously renders the amber 'CV parsing failed. Try again.' alert. Contradictory signals read as a flaky product; the recruiter may wait for parsing that never started. Only fires on an Inngest/network outage (rare).
fix: Return a discriminator from uploadCVAction (e.g. { ok: true, queued: false }) on the dispatch-failure path, mirroring retryParseAction which already returns ok:false there (actions.ts:317); have cv-upload.tsx show a neutral/warning toast ('CV saved, but couldn't start parsing — use Try again.') when queued is false.

title: extract-text NonRetriableError embeds truncated library error text (document-derived) that reaches the Inngest dashboard, bypassing PII controls
userImpact: On a corrupt PDF/DOCX the extract-text step throws `new NonRetriableError('extract-text failed: ${name}: ${message}')` with message = err.message.slice(0,500) from unpdf/mammoth, whose error strings interpolate raw bytes/tokens from the malformed file (e.g. 'Illegal character', 'Bad filter name'). The outer catch re-throws this verbatim and Inngest stores it in its third-party run history, so short document-derived fragments leave the controlled path. Low entropy and only on genuinely corrupt files; no demo impact.
fix: In src/lib/inngest/functions/parse-cv.ts (~line 205) throw `extract-text failed: ${name}` only; if message detail is needed for debugging, log it as PII-free Sentry tags rather than embedding the raw library message in the error surfaced to Inngest. Matches the R4 wrap pattern used elsewhere in the file.

title: search_candidates RPC interpolates the raw query into ILIKE patterns — '%' and '_' act as wildcards (over-match within the org)
userImpact: search_candidates builds `c.full_name ilike '%' || p_query || '%'` (and same for role/company/location/email). p_query is parameterised (no SQL injection) and the RPC is security-invoker (RLS scopes to org — no cross-tenant leak), but '%'/'_' in the query are treated as LIKE metacharacters: a bare '%' matches every candidate in the org, an '_' matches any char. Confusing results on the headline 'find a candidate in seconds' flow when a query contains those characters. findCandidateByEmail already fixed this same class (uses .eq).
fix: Escape LIKE metacharacters in p_query before interpolation in the migration's RPC body (replace '\\','%','_' and add `escape '\\'` to the ILIKE), or rely on the word_similarity branches for partial matching and drop the raw-ILIKE branch. Ship as a new migration (migrations are append-only).

title: markCandidateFieldsFromCV / bumpCandidateEmbedding update candidates by id only under service-role — latent cross-tenant write if a future caller skips the upstream tenant check
userImpact: No current user-visible impact: the only service-role caller (parse-cv) verifies tenancy via the storage_path boundary check before any write. But these helpers update candidates with `.eq('id', candidateId)` and NO organization_id predicate, unlike upsertCandidateFromLinkedIn and embedCandidateFromLinkedIn which assert org equality. A future service-role caller reusing them without the upstream check would silently write across tenants — a defence-in-depth gap, not an active bug.
fix: Thread organizationId into markCandidateFieldsFromCV (src/lib/db/candidate-cvs.ts:411) and bumpCandidateEmbedding (src/lib/db/candidates.ts:551) and add `.eq('organization_id', organizationId)` to the WHERE clause, mirroring the documented service-role-write rule the LinkedIn embed path already follows. org is already in scope in parse-cv.

title: CV upload validates only the client-reported MIME type — no magic-byte/extension check before the Storage write
userImpact: uploadCVAction gates on ACCEPTED_CV_MIME.has(file.type) where file.type is the browser-declared MIME, and passes the same value as contentType to Storage — so the bucket's allowed_mime_types gate is defeated by the same spoofed value. An authenticated user can stash arbitrary <=10MiB bytes in their own org-prefixed private cvs bucket; parsing then fails gracefully. Within-tenant only (no cross-tenant exposure). Minimal for the demo; worth hardening before public apply-form ingestion widens.
fix: In uploadCVAction (src/app/(app)/candidates/[id]/actions.ts:125) sniff leading bytes (PDF '%PDF-', DOCX zip 'PK\x03\x04') and/or validate filename extension against file.type before the Storage upload, as defence in depth on top of the bucket allowed_mime_types.

title: Racing CV uploads surface a generic 'Couldn't record this CV' instead of the documented re-derive — comment and behaviour diverge
userImpact: version is computed before upload and the inline comment claims the (candidate_id, version) unique constraint 'surfaces racing uploads as a 409 we can re-derive from', but createCandidateCV never inspects the error code or retries: on a 23505 the second upload fails with the vague 'Couldn't record this CV. Please try again.' and the user must manually retry. Only triggers when two near-simultaneous uploads hit the same candidate (rare in a 2-3 person agency); non-destructive.
fix: Either implement the promised re-derive (on a 23505 from createCandidateCV, recompute nextCVVersion and retry the insert once) or correct the comment (actions.ts:148-149) and return a clearer message like 'Another CV was uploaded at the same time — please try again.'

title: Edit-candidate server validation errors are keyed under 'patch' and never reach the form fields — silent no-op on a failed save
userImpact: updateCandidateAction validates against z.object({ id, patch: editCandidateSchema }), so flatten().fieldErrors keys errors as { patch: [...] } not { full_name, email, ... } (verified empirically). The form calls form.setError('patch', ...) but no field named 'patch' exists, so the message is dropped — no inline error, no toast. The client resolver normally catches first, but a stale/edge submission reaching the server clears the spinner with absolutely nothing visible happening. (DB-failure path still toasts correctly.)
fix: In src/app/(app)/candidates/[id]/edit/actions.ts (~line 30) parse editCandidateSchema directly on the patch (and validate id with a separate guard), so flatten().fieldErrors carries real field-name keys that form.setError can target. The create action already does this correctly.

title: CV-extracted years_experience can overflow numeric(4,1) and fail the whole merge UPDATE (silent in Inngest, opaque toast on Accept all)
userImpact: markCandidateFieldsFromCV writes parsed.years_experience_total straight into candidates.years_experience (numeric(4,1), max 999.9) with no clamp; salaries write into integer columns. A model misread (months/hours as years, or a foreign-denomination salary) can exceed the column and fail the single atomic patch UPDATE. In the Inngest path the failed DbResult isn't checked, so parsing is still marked complete and the candidate silently shows NO merged fields; on manual 'Accept all' it surfaces as the opaque 'Couldn't merge CV fields' toast. Rare but the 999.9 cap is genuinely tight.
fix: Clamp/validate before writing in markCandidateFieldsFromCV (src/lib/db/candidate-cvs.ts:340) or the parse step: cap years to ~0–80 and salaries to a sane integer range, dropping out-of-range values rather than letting the whole patch UPDATE fail.

title: Add float has no idempotency guard — repeat submits create identical 'Untitled' float rows
userImpact: addFloatAction inserts a float (job_id=NULL, which the unique constraint deliberately treats as distinct to allow many floats per candidate), and FloatForm disables only while pending. A recruiter who clicks 'Add float' twice in succession gets two identical no-note float rows to manually delete. Largely mitigated because the action toasts and revalidates the list before a second click is realistic; rows are timestamped and trivially deletable; matches the codebase's existing no-content-hash pattern for valid-repeat add-actions.
fix: Optional polish: in src/app/(app)/candidates/[id]/floats/actions.ts (~line 63) add a short-window/no-note dedup short-circuit before inserting, or add an optimistic disable-after-success on FloatForm. If multiple floats are genuinely desired, this is downgradeable to a non-issue.

title: Candidate detail / edit / floats routes have no loading.tsx — blank frozen delay on navigation
userImpact: Only the list route has a loading.tsx skeleton. The detail page does four sequential server fetches (getCandidate — which also writes an audit row — then listCandidateCVs, listApplicationsForCandidate, listCandidateActivities) before first paint, and edit/floats also fetch synchronously, but none of these segments provides a loading.tsx. App Router holds the previous page frozen until the RSC resolves, so clicking a candidate/Edit/Floats feels sluggish compared to the responsive list route, especially on a slow demo network.
fix: Add a minimal loading.tsx under src/app/(app)/candidates/[id]/ (and optionally edit/ and floats/) rendering a header + body skeleton, so navigation shows immediate feedback.

title: Cards pagination Next/Previous always appends view=cards, pinning a list-view user into cards on later desktop load
userImpact: candidate-cards.tsx pageHref unconditionally sets view=cards on every Previous/Next link. The shell renders CandidateCards on any sub-768px viewport regardless of the URL param, so a user who paginates on mobile injects a sticky view=cards into the URL; reopening on desktop (or via a shared link) then shows cards instead of their preferred list. The table's pageHref correctly omits view. No data loss; cosmetic, recoverable via the List toggle; rarely triggers on a desktop demo.
fix: In src/app/(app)/candidates/candidate-cards.tsx:49 drop the forced view=cards from pageHref (mirror candidate-table.tsx:79), or only set it when the user explicitly selected cards on desktop. Let the shell's mobile detection decide the view, not the URL.

title: parse-cv embed step bundles the paid Voyage call with the DB write — a post-embed throw re-charges ai_usage on step retry
userImpact: The 'embed-candidate' step.run wraps getCandidateForEmbedding → embed() (billable Voyage call + record_ai_usage row) → bumpCandidateEmbedding in one step. If anything after embed() throws, Inngest replays the whole body and calls embed() again — a second Voyage charge plus a duplicate ai_usage row — over-reporting per-tenant Voyage spend in /settings/usage and skewing the cost-per-tenant data used for SaaS pricing. Reachability is narrow (bumpCandidateEmbedding returns ok:false rather than throwing, so it requires an unexpected non-result throw); not user-visible. The sibling embed-candidate-from-linkedin.ts already isolates the Voyage call in its own step.
fix: In src/lib/inngest/functions/parse-cv.ts (~line 286) split into step.run('voyage-embed') returning the vector (memoised after success) and a separate step.run('persist-embedding') for bumpCandidateEmbedding, mirroring embed-candidate-from-linkedin.ts so a persist failure replays only the persist step.

title: Hybrid embedding silently degrades to structured-fields-only when a candidate is re-embedded by the batch sweep (raw CV prose dropped)
userImpact: The design is a hybrid embedding (structured summary + raw CV text); the inline parse path achieves it because CV text is still in memory, but the scheduled embed-batch sweep calls candidateEmbeddingText(r, null) and getLatestCVTextForCandidate always returns null (raw CV text is never persisted — only structured extracted_data). So once any watched structured field is edited (invalidate_candidate_embedding NULLs the vector) and the sweep re-embeds, the candidate permanently loses the CV-prose half. A candidate findable by prose like 'offshore wind turbine commissioning' right after upload becomes much harder to find after a later edit, with no signal — the headline 'find the right candidate in seconds' value quietly weakens over time. Fresh uploads still embed fully, so the demo itself isn't broken.
fix: Persist the extracted CV plain text (e.g. a candidate_cvs.extracted_text column) at parse time and have getLatestCVTextForCandidate return it, so the batch sweep can rebuild the full hybrid embedding instead of structured-summary-only.

title: listCandidates semantic branch is dead/duplicate code — never reached by the UI, double-reads org, diverges from /search
userImpact: No direct user impact today: the semantic branch (mode === 'semantic') is unreachable — /candidates never passes mode (defaults to trigram) and /search runs its own inline embed + hybridSearchCandidates. The branch still performs a synchronous Voyage embed in an RSC path, resolves current_organization_id() twice, and hardcodes its own minCosineSimilarity 0.3 separate from /search's MIN_COSINE. Risk is future divergence: a second semantic implementation that can drift in thresholds plus an extra paid query-embed if accidentally enabled.
fix: In src/lib/db/candidates.ts (~line 85) either delete the semantic branch and make /search the single semantic entry point, or refactor so org is resolved once and the branch delegates to the same helper /search uses. Do not ship two semantic implementations.

title: Sending a check-in never clears the client's Dormant flag (last_contacted_at is never bumped)
userImpact: The recruiter opens a Dormant client, sends the AI check-in (email genuinely goes out via Outlook), and the client STILL shows the amber Dormant badge, stays in the dashboard 'Dormant clients' widget and the '+N dormant' header count, and 'Last contacted' never updates. The headline re-engagement loop looks like it did nothing.
fix: In sendOutreachAction, after a successful send, explicitly bump the company within the same service-role write: await service.from('companies').update({ last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString() }).eq('id', clientId).eq('organization_id', profile.organization_id). (Or add an AFTER UPDATE trigger on activities that fires bump_last_contacted_at when kind transitions into 'email'.) The existing revalidatePath('/') + revalidatePath('/clients/[id]') will then refresh the cleared state. Root cause: the send path UPDATEs the email_draft row to kind='email', but the bump trigger is AFTER INSERT only and excludes 'email_draft', so neither the original insert nor the flip ever bumps the column.

title: List-view 'Edit' dropdown links to /clients/[id]/edit which does not exist (hard 404) — and there is no other way to edit a client
userImpact: Clicking 'Edit' on any client row in the list view (the obvious way to fix a typo in a company name) navigates to a Next.js 404 'page not found' screen. Worse, no other route or affordance to edit a client exists anywhere — card view and detail header have no Edit option — so a wrong company name/website/industry/notes can never be corrected through the product.
fix: Preferred: create the missing src/app/(app)/clients/[id]/edit/page.tsx rendering a client form wired to the already-existing (currently dead) updateClientAction in [id]/actions.ts:188 and its validation schema. Minimum for the demo: remove the dead Edit DropdownMenuItem (client-table.tsx:98-100) so there is no broken link.

title: Re-opening the check-in modal (or clicking Retry) fires a fresh paid Sonnet draft each time and inserts a duplicate email_draft activity row — no dedup
userImpact: Closing and reopening the modal, opening the same client from both the dashboard widget AND the detail page, or hitting Retry each spends another billable Sonnet call (~0.55p) and leaves another 'Drafted an email' entry in the client's permanent activity history (and the dashboard 'Recent activity' widget) even though nothing was sent. The audit/relationship timeline pitched as a key feature fills with never-sent draft noise and per-tenant AI cost inflates with no visible reason.
fix: Before dispatching a new draft, call getLatestOutreachDraftAction and reuse a recent (e.g. <10 min) unsent email_draft instead of always re-requesting; or short-circuit requestOutreachDraftAction when a fresh draft exists; and/or make the Inngest write an upsert that replaces the most recent unsent email_draft per company rather than insert-each-time. Optionally filter kind='email_draft' out of the rendered timeline until sent.

title: Check-in email is sent to an auto-picked contact the recruiter is never shown and cannot choose
userImpact: The modal shows only Subject and Body — no To:/recipient line and no picker. The recipient is resolved silently server-side as 'the oldest company contact that has an email'. For a client with multiple contacts the real outbound email can go to the wrong person (e.g. an old junior contact instead of the current decision-maker) and the recruiter never sees who it went to before clicking Send — a relationship-damaging action firing blind.
fix: Surface the resolved recipient in the modal as a read-only 'To: Jane Smith <jane@acme.com>' line (returned by a resolve action or by getLatestOutreachDraftAction), and provide a recipient dropdown when the client has more than one contact with an email. At minimum show the To: line before Send is enabled.

title: Contact emails are stored with original casing but matched case-sensitively, so inbound/outbound emails silently fail to attach to the contact timeline
userImpact: A contact saved as 'Jane@Acme.com' (any uppercase) is stored verbatim, but findContactByEmail lowercases the inbound Outlook address and does an exact .eq('email', ...) match, so it never matches. Emails to/from that contact silently drop out of the client's activity history (the Outlook-sync orphan-skip), leaving an incomplete timeline the recruiter never knows is missing entries.
fix: Lowercase the contact email at the write boundary in createContactAction/updateContactAction (e.g. coerceOptional(parsed.data.email)?.toLowerCase()), matching the candidate apply-form convention that findContactByEmail depends on. Alternatively make findContactByEmail case-insensitive via a lower(email) functional index and ilike. Backfill existing rows to lowercase.

title: Client search results are ordered by last-contacted, not by match relevance, so the best match can be buried below the fold
userImpact: When a recruiter types a client name, the page defaults sort to last_contacted_at and SearchInput never sets sort=similarity, so results are ordered by recency of contact rather than trigram similarity. The exact/closest match can land on page 2 (page size 25), making search feel broken for the 'find in seconds' scenario the product is sold on. (The analogous candidates flow correctly ranks by relevance — this is a regression from the documented pattern.)
fix: In listClients, when q is present and the caller hasn't explicitly chosen a sort, pass p_sort='similarity' to search_clients (or have the RPC default-order by similarity when a query is active). Mirror the candidates pattern and disable sort headers while a query is active.

title: Service-role activity flip discards its PostgREST error — email sends but the timeline can silently keep showing it as an un-sent draft
userImpact: After a successful Outlook send, the service-role UPDATE that flips email_draft->email never reads its { error }. supabase-js resolves (does not throw) on a query error, so a failed flip (transient connectivity/RLS/constraint) still returns { ok: true } and the activity row stays kind='email_draft'. The recruiter sees a 'draft' for an email that already went out, gets no error signal, and may re-send — a duplicate outreach email to a real client contact.
fix: Capture and check the result: const { error: flipErr } = await service.from('activities').update(...)...; if (flipErr) Sentry.captureException(flipErr, { ...org/user context }). Don't fail the action (the send already succeeded) but log the divergence so it is diagnosable; consider returning a soft warning.

title: Draft poll freshness window compares the browser clock to the server timestamp — clock skew can make the AI draft never appear (spurious timeout)
userImpact: The modal seeds seedTimeRef = browser Date.now() and accepts a draft only if its server-written occurred_at + 5s >= seed. If the user's laptop clock runs sufficiently ahead of the server, a freshly generated, correct draft is rejected on every poll and the modal lands on 'The AI draft is taking longer than expected' with Retry — which re-seeds an even later client time and deterministically times out again, burning another Sonnet call. The headline AI feature looks broken, per-machine and unrecoverable from the UI. (Two of three verifiers note modern NTP-synced laptops rarely trigger this.)
fix: Don't gate freshness on a client-vs-server timestamp comparison. requestOutreachDraftAction / the Inngest function already return the new draft's activity_id — poll for that specific id (which differs from any pre-existing draft) instead of a time window, removing the clock dependency entirely.

title: Send check-in opens and generates a paid AI draft even when the client has no contact email — fails only at the Send step
userImpact: The detail-page check-in button renders for any client regardless of contacts, and the modal fires a Sonnet draft (real cost) immediately on open. Only at Send does it discover there is no contact email and dead-end with 'No contact with an email on file for this client. Add a contact first.' On a brand-new/contact-less client the recruiter burns an AI draft, edits an email, and only then learns sending is impossible.
fix: Resolve a sendable recipient BEFORE drafting: have requestOutreachDraftAction (or a cheap pre-check) verify a contact-with-email exists and short-circuit with the 'Add a contact first' message before firing the Inngest/Sonnet draft. Optionally hide/disable the Send check-in button when the client has no contact email.

title: Send flips the most-recent draft row rather than the specific draft the recruiter reviewed, leaving stray un-flipped draft rows
userImpact: sendOutreachAction re-queries the newest email_draft (order by occurred_at desc limit 1) and flips that, with no linkage to the activity_id the modal displayed. The emailed body comes from form state so the content sent is correct, but when duplicate draft rows exist (see the no-dedup issue) older drafts are left dangling as 'Drafted an email' entries, cluttering the audit trail.
fix: Pass the reviewed draft's activity_id from the modal into sendOutreachAction and flip that exact row (still scoped to organization_id), tying the sent row to the approved content and avoiding flipping the wrong row when duplicates exist.

title: Client website is rendered as a clickable href with no URL-scheme validation (stored DOM XSS / dangerous-link)
userImpact: The website field is validated only as a trimmed string (no .url(), no scheme allowlist) and rendered as <a href={client.website}>. A same-org user can save 'javascript:...' as a website; a colleague who clicks the link on the detail page executes attacker-controlled script in their authenticated session. RLS-scoped so not cross-tenant, and exploitation needs a malicious same-org user for the 2-3 person anchor — but it becomes a real abuse path in the multi-user SaaS the same codebase is sold as.
fix: Validate the scheme at the schema boundary (require http(s)://) in both create and edit schemas, and guard at render time using the existing sanitiseUrl() helper in src/lib/email/escape.ts — refuse to render the anchor unless client.website starts with http:// or https://, falling back to plain text.

title: deleteContactAction deletes a contact by id without verifying it belongs to the route's company (same-org cross-company delete)
userImpact: deleteContact runs DELETE WHERE id = contactId with no company_id predicate. RLS scopes it to the org (so NOT cross-tenant), but it does not enforce that the contact belongs to the company in the URL — unlike the edit page, which deliberately checks company_id. A crafted/stale request from /clients/A can delete a contact owned by client B in the same org, and the post-delete revalidatePath targets the wrong client's page so the deletion is invisible where the user was looking. Not reachable via the normal UI.
fix: Scope the delete to the company: pass companyId into deleteContact and add .eq('company_id', companyId), or fetch the contact first and assert contact.company_id === companyId before deleting (mirroring the edit page). Return not_found/error otherwise.

title: Single-character client search silently returns the full unfiltered client list with no feedback
userImpact: listClients only runs the search RPC when q.length >= 2; a 1-char query falls through to a plain select returning ALL clients while ?q=a still shows in the URL and the count reads e.g. '1-25 of 40'. The page's isNoMatch flag never fires, so there is no 'keep typing' hint — the first keystroke appears to do nothing. Transient (300ms debounce self-corrects on the 2nd char).
fix: When q is present but below 2 chars, render a subtle 'Type at least 2 characters to search' hint (pass the min-length state to the page so messaging matches the filter applied), or run the search for 1-char queries too.

title: Disabled Previous/Next pagination buttons render with no disabled styling (look clickable)
userImpact: On page 1 / the last page, the disabled branch renders a plain <span> with the disabled prop spread onto it; Tailwind's disabled: styles only match real form controls, so the span gets neither dimming nor pointer-events-none. Previous/Next look identical to active buttons; clicking does nothing (inert span, no misnavigation) with no greyed-out cue. Only appears when total > 25.
fix: On the disabled branch render the span with explicit muted classes (aria-disabled='true' opacity-50 pointer-events-none cursor-not-allowed) instead of relying on the disabled prop, which is a no-op for non-form children under asChild.

title: List/detail 'Dormant' row badge and the 'N dormant' header count use different definitions
userImpact: The per-row amber Dormant badge (isDormant: last_contacted_at older than 60 days) ignores placement history, but the header '+N dormant' count and dashboard widget call the dormant_clients RPC which additionally requires a prior placed application. A client contacted 90+ days ago with no placement shows the amber badge but is excluded from the count and widget — reads as a counting bug and erodes trust in the dormancy feature. (The placement gate was an intentional RPC design choice; the row badge was just never aligned to it.)
fix: Pick one canonical definition: either drop the placement requirement from the RPC count, or have isDormant()/the row badge also require placement history, so the per-row badge and the aggregate count always agree. Document which definition is canonical.

title: Notes and Activity tabs silently cap at 100 entries; older notes disappear with no truncation indicator
userImpact: The detail page fetches getClientTimeline(supabase, id, 100) and the Notes tab is derived by filtering that same 100-row slice. Because the 100 cap is applied across all kinds/entities (calls, emails, job activity) BEFORE the notes filter, an established client with >100 combined activities silently loses older company notes from the Notes tab with no 'showing latest 100' affordance or pagination — a recruiter may conclude a note was never saved. Notes are persisted correctly; only the view truncates. Low incidence for a 2-3 person agency.
fix: Paginate/lazy-load the timeline, query notes separately without the shared 100 cap, or render a clear 'showing the 100 most recent activities' affordance so truncation is visible.

title: Teammate email addresses are shipped to the browser for every timeline entry though only the name is displayed
userImpact: getClientTimeline selects '*' from the client_activity_timeline view (which exposes actor_email) for all 100 rows, and the full timeline plus a mapped { full_name, email } are serialized into a Client Component's props, so org members' emails reach the page source/network payload even when only names render. Same-org internal PII (not cross-tenant), low risk for the anchor; tightens for SaaS tenants. Note: email is used as a fallback when full_name is null, so it can't simply be dropped without handling that case.
fix: Only pass full_name (and actor_user_id) into the client-facing entries unless email is actually rendered; if the null-name fallback needs it, strip actor_email both in the mapper and from the raw timeline prop in page.tsx before passing it down.

title: When Anthropic is down the modal shows a generic 'taking longer than expected' instead of the required 'AI temporarily unavailable' degradation
userImpact: On a real Anthropic outage draftOutreachEmail throws, the Inngest function retries and fails, and no email_draft row is ever written. The modal cannot distinguish 'AI is down' from 'AI is slow' — it just exhausts its 10 polls and shows the timeout/Retry message, and retrying re-fires the whole pipeline into the same outage. Violates the CLAUDE.md contract that AI errors show 'AI temporarily unavailable'. Contained to one optional widget; rest of the app works.
fix: Surface the Inngest run failure to the poller (e.g. write a lightweight 'draft_failed' marker row/status the action can read) so the modal shows 'AI temporarily unavailable — please try again shortly' distinctly from the slow path, and avoid implying a retry will help mid-outage.

title: Auto-precomputed match scores never persist: service-role insert into ai_summaries raises at the set_org trigger (NULL org)
userImpact: The headline AI feature is dead on the demo path. precompute-matches-for-job runs under the service-role client (no auth session), so the ai_summaries_set_org BEFORE INSERT trigger calls current_organization_id(), gets NULL from auth.uid(), and raises 'organization_id is required'. upsertMatchSummary returns {ok:false}, which precompute swallows as a no-op and reports success. Every auto-scored match silently fails to write. The matches page promises 'Matches will appear within ~30 seconds' but every candidate shows 'Not scored yet' forever. The client must click 'Explain match' on each card one-by-one (that on-demand path works only because it runs under the recruiter's authenticated session). For a call selling AI-first matching, automatic scoring never demonstrates. Embeddings still persist (they are UPDATEs, so the BEFORE INSERT trigger never fires) — only the ai_summaries INSERT path is broken.
fix: Give upsertMatchSummary an explicit organizationId param and include it in the insert payload (precompute already has organization_id in scope and verifies it against the job). The ai_summaries cross-tenant FK guard still validates candidate_id/job_id belong to that org, so caller-supplied org is safe. Stop relying on the auth-context trigger for the service-role write path. Add an integration test asserting a precompute run produces a persisted ai_summaries row.

title: Placement fee uses parseFloat — UK-formatted '7,500' silently becomes £7, corrupting headline revenue
userImpact: parsedFeePence() does parseFloat(feeGbp), which stops at the first non-numeric char. A recruiter typing the natural UK figure '7,500' parses to 7 and is stored as 700 pence (£7) — a 1000x understatement. '£7500', '7 500', '7500abc' all mis-parse. The server action only validates the already-truncated pence (z.number().int().min(0).max(100_000_000)), so 700 passes every guard. The fee feeds the source-attribution revenue report and recruiter rollups, so the headline revenue number is silently wrong — the worst figure to be wrong in front of a client evaluating the product as system of record.
fix: Strip thousands separators and reject trailing garbage before parsing in placement-modal.tsx: const cleaned = feeGbp.replace(/[\s,£]/g, ''); if (!/^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$/.test(cleaned)) return null; return Math.round(Number(cleaned) * 100). Show an inline validation hint when the field can't be parsed rather than silently disabling Confirm.

title: Adding a candidate to a job shows a success toast but the candidate never appears until a manual reload
userImpact: AddCandidateForm is a client island on the RSC job-detail page. On success it toasts 'Candidate added to pipeline.' and closes the popover but never calls router.refresh(). The server action only calls revalidatePath, which marks the route cache stale but does not re-render the already-mounted client page, so the ApplicationsList directly below stays unchanged. On the demo: recruiter adds a candidate, sees success, but the Applications list does not show the new row — reads as a silent failure. Re-adding then hits the DB unique guard and produces the contradictory 'may already be on this job' error for what looked like a fresh add.
fix: Import useRouter from next/navigation and call router.refresh() in handleSelect after toast.success, matching the established pattern in application-row-actions.tsx (which calls router.refresh() on success for the same table).

title: Shortlist 'Remove' and 'Convert to application' succeed but the row stays on screen
userImpact: ShortlistList is a client component rendering static `rows` from the RSC page. Both remove() and promote() show a success toast but neither calls router.refresh() nor mutates local state; the actions only revalidatePath. After a successful Remove the candidate row is still visible (looks failed); after a successful Convert (which should drop the row out of the shortlist filter as application_type flips shortlist->standard) the row also stays, so the recruiter cannot tell it worked and may click again. The underlying writes are correct; this is the lone mutation surface in jobs/** that omits the router.refresh() the rest of the codebase uses.
fix: Add useRouter and call router.refresh() after both success toasts in shortlist-list.tsx (or optimistically drop the row from local state), mirroring application-row-actions.tsx.

title: Explain match succeeds but the card never updates — toast tells the recruiter to manually refresh
userImpact: explainCandidateMatchAction does the synchronous 3-6s Sonnet call, upserts the summary, and calls revalidatePath, then returns ok. But ExplainButton runs inside useTransition without router.refresh() and shows toast.success('Match explained — refresh to see details'). revalidatePath on an imperatively-invoked action does not re-render the mounted RSC tree, so the card stays on 'Not scored yet' with the Explain button until a manual reload. In the demo this looks broken on the flagship AI feature: click Explain, wait 6 seconds, see 'done', nothing changes. Recruiter assumes failure and re-clicks (the cache-hit branch makes a re-click cheap, but it still erodes trust). The toast copy is itself an admission the card won't auto-update.
fix: Import useRouter and call router.refresh() after the success toast so the RSC re-fetches and the card upgrades in place; change the toast copy to 'Match explained.' This is the universal pattern across 10+ sibling components.

title: PlacementModal 'Notes' field is collected but never sent or stored — silent data loss on the revenue event
userImpact: The Mark-as-placed modal renders a 'Notes (optional)' Textarea bound to `notes` state, but handleConfirm builds the moveApplicationAction payload without it. moveSchema has no placement-notes field (Zod strips unknown keys), moveApplication has no such arg, and the move_application RPC has no p_placement_notes param — repo-wide grep for placement_notes/placementNotes returns zero hits. A recruiter typing start-date/IR35/fee context clicks Confirm, sees 'Placement recorded.', and the note is gone forever with no signal. The structured revenue fields (fee, date, type, currency) do persist correctly — only the optional free-text note is lost, hence medium not high.
fix: Either remove the Notes field from placement-modal.tsx (so it doesn't promise capture it can't deliver), or thread it through: add a p_placement_notes text param via a new migration to move_application, add placementNotes to MoveApplicationArgs + moveSchema in jobs/[id]/actions.ts, and persist it (e.g. on applications or in the activity metadata jsonb the RPC already builds).

title: Saving a generated job ad shows 'Saved to job ads' but the Saved ads section does not update (and masks duplicate saves)
userImpact: AdPanel lives in a Sheet overlay on the job-detail RSC page. onSave/onSaveScore call saveJobAdAction (which only revalidatePaths) and toast 'Saved to job ads.' but never call router.refresh(); the Sheet doesn't navigate, so SavedAdsList on the underlying page (rendered once from listJobAdsForJob) does not gain the new row until a manual reload. The recruiter can't tell whether it saved and may save duplicates — and because D3-33 allows multiple rows with no dedup, each retry creates another row. The sibling saved-ad-row-actions.tsx already calls router.refresh() after delete, so Delete refreshes the list but Save does not.
fix: Call router.refresh() after toast.success in both onSave and onSaveScore in ad-panel.tsx so the parent page re-fetches listJobAdsForJob and the new ad appears (also surfacing accidental duplicate saves).

title: createApplication detects the duplicate (23505) case but returns the same generic 'internal' code, so the add-candidate message is misleading on real errors
userImpact: createApplication branches on Postgres code 23505 (candidate already on this job) but both that branch and the generic fall-through return the identical { ok:false, code:'internal' } — the distinction is dead code, and DbResult's union has no conflict/duplicate variant to carry it. addCandidateToJobAction therefore surfaces the hedged 'Could not add candidate. They may already be on this job.' for every failure, including transient DB errors and cross-tenant FK guard fires — sending the recruiter to look for a duplicate that doesn't exist. The action fails loudly (no silent success, no data issue); the unique-violation case is also the most common trigger, so the wording is usually correct. Wording/UX polish only.
fix: Add a 'conflict' variant to DbResult's code union, return it from the 23505 branch, and have addCandidateToJobAction map only that to 'This candidate is already on this job.' and 'internal' to a retryable 'Something went wrong, please try again.' addToShortlistAction already does this correctly.

title: Match-spend cost ceiling fails open when the spend lookup returns an error
userImpact: explainCandidateMatchAction only blocks the Sonnet call when spendResult.ok && spendResult.data >= ceiling. If the aggregate read errors (returns {ok:false}), the guard short-circuits to false and the call proceeds unmetered, with no action-level Sentry warning (unlike the precompute path, which logs its own fail-open). On the user-triggered, repeatable Explain button a recruiter hammering it while ai_usage reads flake could exceed the £100/month ceiling. ai_usage still records true cost (visibility intact); only cap enforcement is bypassed, and only during an active read failure, so exposure is small. Not a tenant leak or crash.
fix: On the interactive Explain path, treat a failed spend lookup conservatively (allow but capture a Sentry warning, or surface 'try again') so the fail-open is observable, rather than silently spending.

title: Monthly match-spend ceiling silently under-counts beyond PostgREST's 1000-row cap
userImpact: getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth fetches every match_score ai_usage row for the org/month and sums client-side with no .limit()/.range(); config.toml sets max_rows=1000. Once an org exceeds ~1000 match_score rows in a month, the select truncates to the first 1000, the sum under-reports month-to-date spend, and the spend >= ceiling guard in both precompute-matches-for-job and explainCandidateMatchAction never trips. At ~1-3p/row against a £100 (10,000p) cap, ~3k-10k calls are needed to reach the ceiling, so the cap is defeatable at high volume. ai_usage remains the authoritative ledger (overspend is still visible on the usage dashboard); only automatic enforcement fails. Invisible for a 2-3 person anchor demo; matters for the SaaS-scale cost-cap story.
fix: Replace the row-fetch+JS-sum with a Postgres sum(cost_pence) RPC (e.g. sum_match_spend_this_month(org)) — exact, single-round-trip, immune to the row cap. The code comment already flags this as needed at scale; do it before onboarding a busy tenant.

title: deleteJobAction classifies RPC failures by substring-matching raw free-text error messages
userImpact: deleteJobAction classifies the delete_job outcome via error.message.includes('job_has_applications') and includes('job not found'). The migration raises those exact strings today so it works, but it is brittle: any future reword of the raise text (or Postgres wrapping the message) silently degrades the actionable 'remove all candidates from the pipeline first' guidance into the generic 'Couldn't delete this job' fallback, leaving a recruiter retrying fruitlessly. The block guard itself (re-asserts organization_id = current_organization_id(), blocks on applications) is correctly implemented and tenant-safe; no current user-facing defect. Note all three sibling delete RPCs share SQLSTATE P0001, so branching on error.code alone won't disambiguate.
fix: Have delete_job raise per-case with distinct custom SQLSTATEs (raise exception using errcode = '<distinct code>', message = '...') and branch on those codes rather than substring-matching free text; apply the same to delete_candidate/delete_company.

title: 'Withdrawn' terminal stage has no UI path — candidates can be Rejected but never Withdrawn
userImpact: The application_stage enum and the move-action validation both support 'withdrawn' as a terminal stage (requiring a decline reason), but PIPELINE_STAGES excludes it, and every move control (PipelineCard, ApplicationRowActions, mobile Sheet) iterates PIPELINE_STAGES.filter(s => s !== currentStage), so 'withdrawn' never appears as a destination — the only terminal action is 'Reject…' (DeclineModal hard-codes toStage:'rejected'). When a candidate pulls out (a common event), the recruiter must mis-record it as a Rejection with decline_reason='candidate_withdrew', muddying rejection-vs-self-withdrawal analytics. Working workaround exists; nothing errors.
fix: Either expose a 'Withdraw…' action (analogous to Reject, setting toStage:'withdrawn' with a withdrawal reason) in the card/row dropdowns, or document that withdrawal is intentionally folded into Reject so it isn't perceived as a missing feature.

title: Moving a card out of 'placed' (or out of a terminal decline) leaves stale fee/placement/decline data on the row
userImpact: move_application only sets placement fields when moving into 'placed' and decline fields when moving into 'rejected'/'withdrawn'; every other transition preserves the existing value (else fee_pence / else decline_reason). 'placed' is a rendered kanban column and the 'Move to stage' submenu lists all 7 stages, so moving a placed card back to 'offer' leaves fee_pence/placed_at/placement_type/placement_currency populated on a non-placed row. All current revenue/source consumers re-filter on stage='placed', so no ghost revenue surfaces today — but candidate-applications.tsx renders decline_reason unconditionally, and any future report/export reading fee_pence/decline_reason without re-checking stage would show ghost revenue or a phantom decline reason. Latent integrity/hygiene defect.
fix: In a new append-only migration, in the move_application UPDATE, NULL the placement fields when moving to any non-'placed' stage and NULL the decline fields when moving to any non-terminal stage (else-branch sets NULL rather than preserving), keeping the row consistent with its stage.

title: precompute-matches counters (scored/cache_hits) under-report after any Inngest step retry
userImpact: cacheHits and scored are plain closure variables mutated inside per-candidate step.run() blocks. Inngest memoizes completed step outputs across retries, so on a replay the already-completed score-<id> steps return memoized results without re-executing the closure body, and the ++ never runs for those candidates. After any transient failure that triggers a function retry (the function has retries:3), the returned { scored, cache_hits } is lower than reality. No user-facing impact — these counters only appear in the Inngest return value used for observability — but anyone using them to confirm 'did precompute score N candidates' is misled after a retry.
fix: Return a per-candidate outcome ('hit'|'scored'|'skipped') from each step.run and aggregate the memoized return values after the loop, or document the counters as best-effort/non-authoritative.

title: Matches page hard-errors full-page when current_organization_id() returns null despite a successful getJob
userImpact: After getJob succeeds (so RLS already resolved the org), the page calls current_organization_id() again; if it returns non-string it does a full-page red early return ('Couldn't resolve your organisation. Please refresh.') with no Sentry logging, discarding the successfully-fetched job context. This is inconsistent with the rest of the page, which degrades to inline 'errored' banners within the normal chrome. The path is near-unreachable (a genuine null org would have made getJob return zero rows -> notFound earlier), so it only fires on a transient second-call RPC flake. Rare, low blast radius, pure UX-consistency nit.
fix: Log the unexpected null to Sentry (org resolution failed despite getJob succeeding) and render the existing 'Couldn't load matches' inline banner within the normal page chrome rather than replacing the whole page.

title: Ad generation has no cache check or per-purpose spend ceiling — every 'Generate ad' click re-bills a fresh Sonnet call
userImpact: generateAdAction calls generateAdWithInclusivity (Sonnet 4.6, ~1.8p/call) unconditionally on every button press, with no prior cache/dedup lookup and no per-org monthly spend guard on purpose='ad_generate' (unlike match_score, which both caches in ai_summaries and enforces a ceiling). A recruiter regenerating an ad repeatedly burns tokens unbounded. Generating fresh copy on demand is intentional (caching would be a UX regression here), and each click is a human-rate-limited 3-6s round-trip costing pennies, so the genuine gap is the missing spend ceiling. Acceptable for the demo.
fix: Before charging customers, apply the same getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth-style monthly ceiling to purpose='ad_generate' so a runaway loop is bounded; optionally reuse the most recent saved job_ad when the job's embed-relevant columns are unchanged.

title: Stale match cards still render the OLD Sonnet explanation while only labelling it 'Refreshing…'
userImpact: When a cached summary's candidate/job embedding_version no longer matches the live versions (CV or JD re-embedded), the card sets isStale=true and shows a small amber 'Refreshing…' badge but still renders the full prior content (score, strengths, gaps, screening questions) computed against the outdated data. listMatchSummariesForJob doesn't filter on version, and a candidate-side CV update never auto-re-scores (only job-creation/JD-change does), so the stale row can persist up to ~7 days until the recruiter clicks Explain or the weekly cleanup cron deletes it. A recruiter could relay now-incorrect strengths/gaps/screening questions to a client with only an easy-to-miss badge as warning. Content was valid when generated and there is a visible signal, so low.
fix: When isStale, de-emphasise the body (opacity + a clear 'This explanation is out of date — click Explain to refresh' banner) or hide strengths/gaps/screening-questions and show only the staleness prompt, so stale AI content can't be mistaken for current; correct the misleading 'next precompute run will replace it' comment for the candidate-stale case.

title: Match scoring degrades silently when the spend ceiling is hit — no UI signal that AI is paused
userImpact: When precompute detects the org is at/over the monthly spend ceiling it returns early with only a Sentry warning and leaves the cache unpopulated, so the matches page shows blank 'Not scored yet' cards with no 'scoring paused' state — the page never reads getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth. The on-demand Explain action does surface a clear 'Match scoring is paused this month…' toast, but a recruiter who never clicks Explain sees only blank cards with no on-screen reason scoring was deliberately paused. Graceful degradation (vector top-N still renders, Explain still works); the WHY is just invisible. Default ceiling is £100/month, so a fresh demo is unlikely to trip it unless the cap is set artificially low.
fix: Surface a banner on the matches page when month-to-date spend is at the ceiling (the page can read getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth) explaining scoring is paused for the month, reusing the toast wording already in explainCandidateMatchAction.

title: Free-text currency from a spec call crashes the job detail page (Intl.NumberFormat RangeError)
userImpact: A recruiter approves a spec call whose currency was transcribed/typed as '£', 'pounds' or 'GBP ' (trailing space). A job is created, but clicking into it throws a RangeError during server render and shows the (app) error boundary ('Something went wrong') instead of the job. The job is un-viewable until the bad value is fixed directly in the DB — there is no in-app currency edit field. Highly plausible in a live demo because the value originates from AI transcription of a verbally-stated salary.
fix: Make formatSalary defensive: wrap the new Intl.NumberFormat construction in src/app/(app)/jobs/[id]/job-detail-header.tsx:28 in try/catch and fall back to 'GBP' (or plain number + raw code) so one bad value can never crash the page. Also constrain currency to ISO-4217 (uppercase 3-letter regex/enum) in structuredJdSchema (src/app/(app)/spec/[id]/review/actions.ts:45) and coerce-to-GBP-when-invalid at job creation in create-job-from-spec.ts:184.

title: Approve marks the draft 'approved' BEFORE firing the Inngest event; a transient send failure leaves an already-approved draft showing a destructive 'failed' error toast with no forward path
userImpact: On an Inngest cold start / transient dispatch failure, the three DB writes (company_id, structured_data, status='approved') have already committed, but the action returns ok:false. The form (spec-review-form.tsx:81-84) treats any !ok as a hard failure: red error toast, no navigation. The recruiter is told it failed while the row is genuinely approved, and is stranded on the review page. The documented recovery (re-click Approve) re-fires job creation.
fix: Fire inngest.send BEFORE marking approved (so a send failure leaves the draft cleanly un-approved and re-clickable), OR return a distinct ok:true-with-warning shape so the form navigates to /jobs and shows a non-destructive 'Approved — job is being created' toast instead of implying nothing happened.

title: Approve navigates to /jobs and relies on a single fixed 2s setTimeout refresh; on Inngest cold start the async job insert isn't done, so the recruiter lands on a /jobs list missing the job they just created
userImpact: After approve, the form does router.push('/jobs') + a single setTimeout(router.refresh, 2000). The jobs row is inserted asynchronously by create-job-from-spec (Inngest dispatch + 3 sequential step round-trips), which routinely exceeds 2s on a cold start — exactly the first-approve-of-a-session demo scenario. The recruiter gets a 'Draft approved — creating job…' success toast, lands on Jobs, and their new job is missing with no spinner and no further refresh. They assume it failed (and may re-approve). Self-heals only on a manual reload.
fix: Replace the one-shot 2s refresh. Either keep the recruiter on a 'Creating job…' state that polls the draft's created_job_id (already patched by create-job-from-spec.ts:203) and deep-links to /jobs/[id] once set, or poll the jobs list a few times. The status-poller pattern already exists at review/page.tsx:44-47 for the transcribe lifecycle and is idiomatic to reuse.

title: Per-card delete button on the spec list is a one-click destructive soft-delete with no confirmation (sibling Reject button DOES confirm)
userImpact: The small ghost trash icon next to each card fires deleteSpecDraftAction -> markSpecDraftRejected (status='rejected', rejected_at, deleted_at=now) on a single click with no confirm()/dialog, unlike the in-review Reject button which confirms (spec-review-form.tsx:95). A mis-tap instantly removes a transcribed/structured spec call from the list (and starts the 30-day hard-delete + audio-purge clocks) with only a post-hoc 'Draft deleted.' toast and no in-app undo. This is the lone unguarded destructive delete in the app (candidates/clients/jobs all confirm) and violates the global rule that destructive actions require confirmation.
fix: Add a window.confirm() (or shadcn AlertDialog) before deleteSpecDraftAction in spec-delete-button.tsx, mirroring the review form's reject ('Delete this draft? The recording will be deleted in 30 days.'), and/or offer an Undo on the success toast.

title: Review page is a dead-end when the tenant has no clients — approve is permanently disabled with no link/inline way to add a client
userImpact: Client selection is required to approve (button disabled when !companyId; the server action also hard-rejects a null company). When the tenant has zero clients the form shows plain helper text 'No clients yet — add one in Clients first, then return here.' with no link and no inline-create. A brand-new agency (the exact anchor/demo scenario) records and transcribes their first spec call, reaches review, and finds the only enabled button is 'Reject draft' — the only forward action is impossible, and navigating away to create a client discards their edits.
fix: Turn the helper text into an actionable link to /clients/new (the route exists) or render an 'Add a client' button, ideally opening the new-client flow in a dialog so the review form state survives.

title: Contract/temp day rates from spec calls are written into the annual salary columns and rendered as an annual salary
userImpact: jd-extract tells Sonnet salary_range_min/max is 'annual for perm, daily rate for contract', but create-job-from-spec unconditionally writes that value into jobs.salary_min/max regardless of job_type and never populates jobs.day_rate_min/max (dead columns). The job detail header only formats salary_min/max. So a contract role with a £500/day rate is stored and shown as '£500' next to a 'Contract' badge — a glaring UK-recruitment domain error a client will immediately spot, undermining the demo's day-rate/charge-rate credibility.
fix: Branch on jd.job_type in the insert payload (create-job-from-spec.ts): for 'contract'/'temp', write salary_range_min/max into day_rate_min/day_rate_max and leave salary_min/max null. Render day rate with a '/day' suffix for contract/temp in job-detail-header.tsx, and relabel the inputs in spec-review-form.tsx accordingly.

title: No unique/atomic idempotency guard on create-job-from-spec — a double-delivered or re-clicked 'approved' event can insert TWO jobs for one spec draft
userImpact: The only guard is a read-then-check of draft.created_job_id, which is written in a LATER step (patch-draft-job-id) AFTER the unconditional jobs insert. Concurrency is keyed on organization_id (not draft id), there is no DB unique constraint tying a job to a spec draft, and Inngest delivers at-least-once with no event id for dedup. Two deliveries (at-least-once redelivery, or the approve action's documented re-click recovery path) that both read before either patches will both insert a separate jobs row — the same role listed twice in /jobs, with downstream duplicate applications/placements. Notably the approve action's own comment claims a 'where created_job_id is null' guard that does not actually exist.
fix: Make creation atomically idempotent: gate the spec_drafts patch with `.is('created_job_id', null)` and only keep the inserted job if it affected a row (else delete/skip), OR add a unique (organization_id, spec_draft_id) on jobs with on-conflict, OR set an Inngest idempotency key on spec_draft_id so duplicate deliveries collapse to one run.

title: Spec-call transcript (names, salaries, client detail) is retained indefinitely on approved drafts — the 30-day retention sweep clears the audio but never the transcript
userImpact: The retention sweep deletes the Storage audio object and NULLs audio_storage_path but never touches spec_drafts.transcript, the full verbatim text of the call (hiring-manager names, candidate names, salary figures). Approved drafts are never hard-deleted (only rejected/soft-deleted rows are vacuumed), so an approved draft keeps its raw PII transcript forever — contradicting the stated 30-day audio-retention promise and the audit-ready/bounded-retention mandate. GDPR data-minimisation exposure and a SaaS-portability liability, though not user-facing at demo time.
fix: In spec-audio-retention-sweep.ts, also NULL the transcript column (and optionally structured_data) when removing audio for rows past the 30-day window, OR explicitly document and add a deliberate transcript-retention boundary to the retention policy.

title: Whisper transcribes and bills the FULL recording before the >60-minute duration guard rejects it — unbounded per-call AI spend
userImpact: The only server-side caps are byte size (100 MiB, ~3-3.5h of audio) and mime; the 60-minute cap is a client-only UI hint. Duration is known only from Whisper's verbose_json AFTER the full file is decoded, billed, and logged to ai_usage. So a 2-3 hour file (or a malicious SaaS-tenant upload) is fully transcribed and paid for (~£0.85-£1.15), then rejected to the user with 'over 60 minutes'. The per-tenant ledger shows spend with no resulting draft; the owner cannot bound cost per spec call. Violates the CLAUDE.md AI-cost-visibility/boundedness constraint.
fix: Probe duration BEFORE the Whisper call: run ffprobe against the recompressed /tmp file inside recompressToOpus (ffprobe is reliable on a seekable file, unlike a stream) and reject >MAX_DURATION_SECONDS pre-transcription. Also fix the misleading 'instead of paying Whisper to transcribe noise' comment.

title: Whisper transcript is discarded when the later Sonnet JD step fails — forces a full audio re-upload and re-pay for transcription
userImpact: The transcript is only written to spec_drafts.transcript in persist-draft, which runs AFTER sonnet-structure-jd. If extractJdFromTranscript exhausts its retries (Claude overloaded), the function throws, the draft is marked 'failed', and the transcript is never saved. Because structured_data.title is empty, the status page offers only 'Upload another recording', so the recruiter must re-upload the audio and re-run ffmpeg + Whisper + re-bill — even though transcription succeeded. Violates core principle 4 (cache AI outputs; regenerate only on material change).
fix: Persist the transcript in its own step immediately after Whisper succeeds, before the Sonnet step. On Sonnet failure, keep status='failed' but retain the transcript and offer a 'retry extraction' CTA that calls only Sonnet, not the full audio pipeline.

title: Approve shows full success even though the async job insert can still fail downstream (e.g. missing-company / insert error) with no path back to the recruiter
userImpact: The form declares success at inngest-send time and navigates to /jobs. If create-job-from-spec later NonRetriably fails (missing company flips the row to status='failed'; or a hard insert error), the recruiter has already been told it worked and is no longer on /spec, where the only surfacing lives. No toast/notification/poll on /jobs. The missing-company branch is effectively dead from the UI (pre-checked), so this fires only on rare hard insert failures (which leave status='approved' with no parse_error), making it unlikely in a normal demo.
fix: Keep the recruiter on a 'Creating job…' state that polls created_job_id (success → link to job) or status='failed' (show parse_error), rather than declaring success at send-time. Largely subsumed by the 2s-refresh-race fix.

title: getSpecDraft has no deleted_at filter — a soft-deleted (rejected) draft is still fetchable by URL and can be re-approved, resurrecting a discarded draft into a live job
userImpact: listSpecDrafts hides soft-deleted rows but getSpecDraft does a bare .eq('id').maybeSingle() with no deleted_at guard. After rejecting a draft, navigating back (browser back / bookmark) lands on the review page, which still renders the editable form and Approve button for a rejected (deleted_at-set) draft. Approve has no status/deleted_at check, nor does create-job-from-spec, so re-approving flips status to 'approved' and inserts a real job — and leaves a contradictory approved+deleted_at row the 30-day cleanup sweep will later hard-delete out from under the live job. Same-tenant only (RLS blocks cross-org), and requires a specific back-navigate-then-Approve sequence.
fix: Add an `includeDeleted` arg to getSpecDraft (default false) applying `.is('deleted_at', null)` and return not_found for soft-deleted rows on the poller/review pages, and/or redirect away when draft.status==='rejected' || draft.deleted_at. Ideally also add status/deleted_at guards in approveSpecDraftAction and create-job-from-spec.

title: Approving overwrites structured_data with only the form fields, silently wiping the AI confidence_per_field map and 'verify with client' ambiguities
userImpact: updateSpecDraftStructuredData does a full jsonb replace with the ~13 editable fields only (approveSchema has no confidence_per_field/ambiguities and Zod strips unknowns). The Sonnet-produced low-confidence flags and 'verify with client' checklist are erased on first approve. Approved drafts remain reachable at /review, so re-opening one to re-consult the verify list (or to retry after a failed job creation) shows it blank — losing the exact AI value-add. The data is correct during the review-before-approve step (the path a demo exercises), so impact is on the secondary re-open/retry path.
fix: Read-modify-write: merge the recruiter edits over the existing structured_data instead of replacing it, OR carry confidence_per_field/ambiguities through approveSchema, OR persist those two keys separately so they survive an approve/retry cycle.

title: Spec list silently renders the 'record your first spec call' empty state when the DB query errors
userImpact: The page does `const drafts = result.ok ? result.data : []`, so any internal error (RLS hiccup, connection failure) collapses to an empty array and renders the onboarding EmptyState. A recruiter with 20 drafts who hits a transient error is told they have none — alarming and misleading. The error is captured to Sentry but invisible in-app. Sibling pages (pipeline, jobs/[id]/matches) handle their primary-query !ok with a distinct error state; this page violates that convention.
fix: On !result.ok, throw (let (app)/error.tsx handle it) or render a distinct inline error ('Couldn't load your spec calls — refresh to try again') so a fetch failure is never mistaken for 'no data', matching the pipeline/matches pattern.

title: Status page uses a meta-refresh full-page poller (4s white flash); the failed-without-JD path leaves a stale 'Failed' card and only offers 'Upload another'
userImpact: While pending/transcribing, the page renders <meta http-equiv='refresh' content='4'>, forcing a full document reload with a visible white flash every 4s for the entire 30-60s transcription wait — jarring in a demo. On a hard failure the only primary CTA is 'Upload another recording'; the failed draft is never auto-deleted, so it lingers as a 'Failed' card the recruiter must manually trash. Purely cosmetic/UX, no data loss.
fix: Replace the meta-refresh with a small client poller (useEffect + router.refresh on an interval) so the page updates in place without a flash; on the failed path also link back to /spec, not only /spec/new.

title: Audio object is left orphaned in Storage when inngest.send fails (and on every other 'failed' path) — never rolled back, never swept
userImpact: On inngest.send failure the action marks status='failed' and returns an error but, unlike the updateSpecDraftAudioPath branch, does NOT remove the uploaded audio object. The retention sweep filters status in ('approved','rejected') and the cleanup sweep keys on deleted_at, so 'failed' drafts' audio is never reclaimed. Worse, the more common pipeline failures (markSpecFailed) also leave audio orphaned. Recorded PII audio persists in Storage indefinitely past the intended 30-day boundary, accruing cost. Invisible to the operator; not demo-facing.
fix: On the inngest.send failure path, remove the object (supabase.storage.from('spec-audio').remove([storagePath])) mirroring the line-163 rollback, AND include 'failed' rows in the audio retention sweep so all failure paths are eventually reclaimed.

title: Salary range from the review form has no min<=max check and no upper bound — reversed ranges render nonsensically, oversized integers cause a silent post-success job-insert failure
userImpact: salary_range_min/max are z.number().int().min(0) with no .max() and no cross-field refine. A reversed range (min=80000, max=60000) is accepted and shown as '£80,000 – £60,000'. A value above the Postgres int max passes Zod but throws on insert; because create-job-from-spec's onFailure only logs to Sentry (it does NOT set status='failed'), the recruiter sees a success toast and redirect, no job ever appears, and there is no user-facing error — even more silent than the report's 'transcription failed' framing. Both require deliberately malformed manual entry; AI extraction would essentially never produce them. Inconsistent with jobs/new which enforces min<=max and caps at 10M.
fix: Add .max(2_000_000_000) (or a realistic cap) to both fields and a .superRefine asserting min<=max with a clear inline message before approval, matching src/app/(app)/jobs/new/schema.ts.

title: Extracted/edited seniority_level is never persisted onto the created job
userImpact: The review form captures seniority_level and saves it into spec_drafts.structured_data, but create-job-from-spec's insert payload never writes it (jobs has no seniority_level column — it lives on candidates) and composeDescription omits it from the description text. So a recruiter's explicit Senior/Lead selection silently evaporates on the created job ('I entered that, where did it go?'). No data loss (it persists on the draft); cosmetic gap, often also encoded in the title.
fix: Include seniority_level in composeDescription (e.g. 'Seniority: senior') so it survives on the job description, or add a job field if/when one exists.

title: MicRecorder can emit a 0-byte recording (stop with no chunks) that previews as 'Recording captured' but fails upload with a misleading 'Choose an audio file' error
userImpact: rec.onstop builds the File from chunksRef unconditionally; if no non-empty dataavailable fired (near-instant stop, muted/glitchy device), the result is a 0-byte File. The preview still shows 'Recording captured' with '0 KB' and a playable element, and the Upload button enables (a 0-byte File is truthy). On submit the server rejects size===0 with 'Choose an audio file before uploading.' — contradicting the on-screen preview. No bad data is persisted (server guard fires); narrow degenerate path; recoverable by re-recording.
fix: In rec.onstop, if blob.size === 0 (or chunksRef is empty), do not transition to 'preview' — show an inline 'No audio captured — try again' and call onRecording(null) so Upload stays disabled. Optionally give the upload action a distinct 'That recording was empty' message.

title: Sonnet outage produces a generic 'Transcription failed' message instead of graceful 'AI temporarily unavailable' degradation
userImpact: When only the Sonnet JD-extraction step fails (Claude overloaded), the failure path sets parse_error to the generic FAILED_USER_MESSAGE 'Transcription failed. Try uploading again, or contact support.' This is misleading — transcription actually succeeded — and tells the user to re-upload (re-paying Whisper) instead of retrying shortly. Contradicts the documented 'AI temporarily unavailable' graceful-degradation lens. Fires only during a transient outage; no data corruption.
fix: Distinguish the Sonnet-step failure from the transcribe-step failure: set a parse_error like 'AI is temporarily unavailable — your transcript is saved, please retry in a moment.' and offer a retry-extraction CTA (depends on persisting the transcript before the Sonnet step).

title: Whisper always returns durationSeconds >= 1, so the pipeline's 'could not read the audio file' (<=0) guard is dead code and its stale docstring is wrong
userImpact: transcribe() computes durationSeconds = Math.max(1, ...), so it can never return <=0; the consumer's `if (durationSeconds <= 0)` branch and its 'Could not read the audio file. Please re-upload.' message are unreachable dead code, and the whisper.ts docstring ('<=0 → throws') is stale (it clamps). A genuinely unreadable file is still correctly caught by ffmpeg guards / the empty-transcript path, so there is no user-facing misbehaviour — this is a maintainability/dead-code + stale-doc issue, invisible at demo time.
fix: Either drop the Math.max(1, ...) floor so the <=0 guard can fire, or remove the dead -1 branch and fix the stale docstring so the only real guards are over-60-min and empty-transcript.

title: Shared process-wide MSAL singleton can persist another user's Outlook refresh token during concurrent/sequential token refresh
userImpact: Recruiter A's Outlook row can silently store recruiter B's Microsoft refresh token. On A's next sync, A authenticates as B and pulls B's inbox (potentially a different agency/mailbox) onto candidate timelines while breaking A's own connection. Invisible until the wrong emails appear. The 6-hourly cron refreshing every expiring user sequentially in one process is the live trigger.
fix: In getValidAccessToken (outlook.ts:~347), stop picking the rotated RT as 'any cache entry that isn't mine'. Scope the lookup to the current account's home_account_id (match.home_account_id === the user's MSAL homeAccountId) — the correct pattern already exists in exchangeCodeForTokens at outlook.ts:224-226. Or build a fresh ConfidentialClientApplication per refresh, or use a per-user cache plugin.

title: First-time Outlook connect reports success but persists nothing — service-role INSERT relies on a set_org trigger that raises under a session-less client, then the UPDATE fallback no-ops silently
userImpact: On every first-time Outlook connect, upsertOutlookCredentials inserts via the service-role client (no auth session) and relies on the BEFORE INSERT set_organization_id() trigger to fill organization_id — but current_organization_id() is null under service-role, so the hardened trigger raises. The code falls into a service-role UPDATE that matches zero rows (no row exists yet); PostgREST returns error:null for a zero-row UPDATE, so persist_failed is skipped and the handler redirects ?outlook=connected. The recruiter sees a 'Outlook connected' success toast while no credentials row exists and the subscription-create event references a missing row.
fix: In the callback, pass the already-resolved organizationId (route.ts:146) explicitly into upsertOutlookCredentials (outlook-credentials.ts:150-161) so the insert never depends on a session-less trigger — mirroring candidate-cvs.ts/spec-drafts.ts/activities.ts which all pass organizationId for exactly this reason. After the UPDATE fallback, add .select('id') and treat zero affected rows as persist_failed instead of success.

title: Outlook connect requests Mail.Send while the card copy and DB schema both claim read-only access
userImpact: The Connect-Outlook card renders an unconditional 'Read-only access (Mail.Read). We never send email on your behalf.' but OUTLOOK_SCOPES (outlook.ts:55-60) includes Mail.Send and getAuthorizationUrl forces prompt=consent, so Microsoft's consent screen explicitly asks the recruiter to grant 'Send mail as you' — directly contradicting the in-app promise during the demo. It reads as a bug or dark pattern and is a consent-transparency weakness. The migration header + scopes column default (20260519092945_outlook_credentials.sql lines 4-5, 56) likewise document read-only scopes while runtime persists Mail.Send, so the whole stack's documentation disagrees with itself.
fix: Reconcile in one direction. Either update the card copy to state that send (explicitly-triggered, never auto-send) is requested, OR remove 'Mail.Send' from the base OUTLOOK_SCOPES used by getAuthorizationUrl and rely on buildIncrementalConsentUrl (outlook.ts:44-54) for the first send. Then add a migration to update the scopes column default + header comment to match whatever is shipped.

title: Team invite 'Invitation sent'/'resent' toast fires even when no email is actually delivered (no API key / no origin / http_error)
userImpact: inviteMemberAction and resendInviteAction return { ok: true } once the DB row is written, regardless of email outcome. The Resend send is best-effort: no_api_key (RESEND_API_KEY optional in env.ts) is swallowed with zero signal, a null resolveOrigin skips the email entirely, and only http_error logs to Sentry — none surface to the owner. The client shows a confident green 'Invitation sent'/'Invitation resent' while the teammate receives nothing. With custom SMTP flagged as a launch-blocker (MEMORY), this is a realistic demo-night state.
fix: Propagate a soft delivery flag from the action (e.g. { ok: true, emailDelivered: boolean }) so team-invites.tsx (lines 113, 217) can show 'Invitation created — but we couldn't email it (check email settings)' for the no_api_key / skipped-origin / http_error cases. At minimum verify RESEND_API_KEY and NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL are set before the demo.

title: Profile email field is editable and overwrites public.users.email while labelled 'Display only', desyncing displayed identity from the sign-in identity
userImpact: The email Input is fully editable (no readOnly) and updateProfileAction writes whatever is typed straight into public.users.email, while auth.users.email (used to sign in / receive magic links) is never touched. The user edits it, clicks Save, gets a green 'Profile saved' toast, and believes their email changed.
