In reality login is unchanged but the displayed email — used in TopNav, the Team members list, and the inviter label on outbound invites — is now wrong and permanently diverges with no reconciliation. In a demo this looks like a broken/untrustworthy save.
fix: Make the email field readOnly (mirror organization-form.tsx's readOnly={!isOwner} pattern) and drop `email` from updateProfileSchema/updateProfile (profiles.ts:43-46) so the action only saves full_name. Until a real auth-email-change flow ships, the field must not accept edits the copy says are ignored.

title: Outlook reconnect after an org switch keeps a stale organization_id, so the mail subscription is never created and email silently never ingests
userImpact: On reconnect the service-role UPDATE branch rewrites tokens/scopes and clears revoked_at but never updates organization_id (the BEFORE INSERT trigger does not fire on UPDATE). If the user switched orgs via accept_invitation (which moves users between orgs) and the old org still has other members, the credential row keeps the OLD org id while the subscription-create event carries the freshly-read NEW org id. createOutlookSubscription throws NonRetriableError('credentials not in claimed organization'), so the subscription is never created, the 6-hourly cron can't recover it (subscription_expires_at is NULL), and the recruiter sees 'Outlook connected' while emails silently never appear on timelines.
fix: In the reconnect UPDATE branch, also set organization_id to the freshly-resolved organizationId and null subscription_id/subscription_client_state/subscription_expires_at/delta_link. Better: replace the insert-then-manual-update dance with a single upsert onConflict('user_id') that always writes organization_id from the resolved value.

title: Usage page shows £0 spend if the ai_usage query errors — silent-fail swallows the error
userImpact: The ai_usage read destructures only `data` and ignores `error`; on any transient query failure rowsRaw is null, rows becomes [], and the entire dashboard (headline total, by-purpose table, ceiling bar, top-10) renders zero with the copy 'No AI usage recorded yet this month.' — indistinguishable from a genuinely zero-spend org. The owner concludes AI costs nothing / nothing is tracked, defeating the page's purpose as the single source of truth for AI cost.
fix: Destructure `{ data, error }`; on error render an explicit 'Couldn't load usage — try again' state and call Sentry.captureException, rather than collapsing to a zeroed dashboard (matches the project's own error.tsx Sentry norm).

title: Backfill / Build-index buttons never refresh the page — unembedded count and 'Ready to build' state stay stale and the button locks at 'Queued'
userImpact: triggerCandidateBackfillAction/triggerHnswBuildAction fire an Inngest event and return { ok:true } with no revalidatePath, and the buttons set a client-only done=true with no router.refresh. After clicking 'Backfill N candidates' the page keeps showing the same N and a permanently-disabled 'Queued' button until a full manual reload, so the founder can't tell if anything is progressing. The HNSW 'Build queued' toast is also misleading since the real CREATE INDEX is a documented manual DDL step. (Re-firing is effectively idempotent — embed-batch only sweeps unembedded rows.)
fix: After a successful queue, call router.refresh() in the button (or revalidatePath('/settings/integrations') in actions.ts, matching the sibling outlook-actions.ts pattern), add a 'Refresh status' affordance / on-screen note that the count updates shortly, and don't permanently disable the button on a single fire. Clarify the HNSW copy that index creation is a manual step.

title: Per-call Math.ceil rounding inflates embedding/parsing spend on the Usage dashboard
userImpact: calcEmbedCostPence (and calcCostPence in claude.ts) do Math.ceil into the integer cost_pence column, so every sub-penny embed/parse call is rounded up to a whole penny independently and the error accumulates per call. Single-call paths (cv_parse, parse-cv candidate_embed, linkedin_candidate_embed) over-report by up to ~1p each, overstating the displayed monthly total/by-purpose figures. (The headline backfill example is overstated — bulk backfill batches 128 candidates per embed call, so 1,000 candidates is ~8 rows, not 1,000.)
fix: Stop rounding each call to a whole penny: store cost in a finer unit (millipence as numeric) or store raw token counts and compute cost in the dashboard. If the integer column must stay, accumulate fractional pence and round only the displayed monthly aggregate.

title: Match-scoring spend ceiling fails open when the spend lookup errors, allowing unbounded Sonnet spend
userImpact: The Explain action's ceiling guard is `if (spendResult.ok && spendResult.data >= MAX_MONTHLY_MATCH_SPEND_PENCE)`. When getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth returns { ok:false } on a query error, the && short-circuits and the synchronous Sonnet call proceeds unbounded — silently disabling the £100/month cap the Usage page advertises as automatic. Bounded in practice (one manual click = a few pence, only while the aggregate read is failing), but it contradicts its own fix comment and, unlike the precompute path, logs nothing.
fix: Fail closed: treat !spendResult.ok as 'cannot confirm under the cap' and refuse the Sonnet call with the same 'match scoring paused' message plus a Sentry warning (mirroring the deliberate, logged decision in precompute-matches-for-job.ts). Only run the model when spend < ceiling is positively confirmed.

title: Candidate-embedding backfill has no owner role gate (sibling HNSW trigger is owner-only) or server-side idempotency
userImpact: triggerCandidateBackfillAction is org-scoped (no cross-tenant leak) but, unlike its explicitly owner-gated sibling triggerHnswBuildAction, has no users.role check, so any recruiter can queue an org-wide embedding backfill, and the only repeat-fire brake is the client-only done state. An authorization inconsistency rather than a real cost-inflation vector (embed-batch only embeds rows WHERE embedding IS NULL, so repeat fires after the first sweep cost nothing).
fix: Mirror the owner check from triggerHnswBuildAction (SELECT role FROM users WHERE id = user.id; reject non-owners) in triggerCandidateBackfillAction. Optionally add a short server-side de-dupe so reload-spam can't re-queue.

title: Resend on an already-accepted invite errors only after the optimistic 'Resending…' badge, leaving a stale actionable row
userImpact: If a teammate accepts an invite in another tab between page load and click, handleResend optimistically flashes 'Resending…' then resendInviteAction returns the error 'That invitation has already been accepted.' as an error toast. The pending list never calls router.refresh(), so the now-accepted invite lingers with live Resend/Revoke buttons until the next revalidation. Cosmetic multi-tab race; the server guard prevents any wrong action.
fix: On resend/revoke failure call router.refresh() so the stale accepted row drops out of the pending list, and downgrade the accepted-case copy to an informational toast rather than an error.

title: Resend of a near-expired invite swallows a failed expiry-refresh and still reports success
userImpact: resendInviteAction only refreshes expires_at when the invite is within 24h of expiry. If that service-role UPDATE fails, the code deliberately falls through and still re-sends the email against the old (about-to-expire) token and returns { ok:true }, so the owner sees 'Invitation resent' while the teammate's link may die within hours. expires_at is enforced at accept time, so the link genuinely fails — with no signal to the owner that the refresh silently failed.
fix: If the stale-refresh UPDATE fails, retry once or return a soft warning so the UI can say 'resent, but the link still expires soon — try again'. Do not show an unqualified 'Invitation resent' when the intended expiry extension did not happen.

title: Reconnecting Outlook shows the original connection date, not the reconnect date
userImpact: On reconnect the UPDATE path resets revoked_at and overwrites tokens but never touches created_at, and the card renders 'Connected <created_at>'. A recruiter who disconnects and reconnects sees a connection date that predates their actual reconnection — mildly confusing when verifying the integration is freshly working, visible only if reconnect is demonstrated.
fix: On the reconnect UPDATE, also set a dedicated connected_at/reconnected_at timestamp (or set created_at = now() if it is meant to mean 'current connection start') and surface that in the card.

title: Usage 'this month' window is UTC but per-row 'When' timestamps render in ambient (server) timezone, mismatching at month edges
userImpact: monthStart is computed in UTC and the query filters created_at >= monthStart, but per-row dates render via toLocaleString('en-GB') in the ambient timezone. The internal totals all share the UTC window so they reconcile with each other; only a per-row displayed date for a call timestamped within the TZ-offset band on the single day at a month boundary can disagree with which month the totals attribute it to. On Vercel (UTC server) it is effectively invisible.
fix: Pick one timezone basis: compute monthStart in Europe/London (UK product) or render the table timestamps in the same UTC basis as the window so the boundary and displayed dates agree.

title: Apply-form toggle holds local useState that never re-syncs to the server prop after revalidation
userImpact: ApplyFormToggle seeds useState(initialEnabled) and never reconciles with later prop changes, so after revalidatePath('/settings') re-runs the RSC the checkbox can show a value disagreeing with the server. For the single-owner anchor case the optimistic set/revert is correct so no visible desync occurs; impact is limited to rare multi-owner drift until a reload.
fix: Either lift the toggle to a server-action <form> with the server value as source of truth, or add a useEffect that resyncs `enabled` when initialEnabled changes (or key the component on it). Low priority for a single-owner demo.

title: Pending-invite 'time ago' uses unstable per-call now, risking a hydration mismatch and a brief inviter-label flicker
userImpact: TeamInvites (a Client Component) calls formatTimeAgo(row.created_at) with no stable `now`, so the SSR string (server clock) and hydration string (browser clock) can differ across a bucket boundary, emitting a React hydration warning and swapping the text. Separately the optimistic add shows 'Invited by you' then the resolved name. Cosmetic flicker on load; dev-only console warnings.
fix: Pass a single stable `now` captured once (useState(() => new Date()) or a server prop) into formatTimeAgo within the client component, and keep the optimistic inviterLabel consistent with the resolved label.

title: Source-attribution report crashes on render once any placement exists (numeric/bigint arrives as string, .toFixed undefined)
userImpact: The only report linked from the /reports hub throws a TypeError and is replaced by the generic error screen the instant the agency has any placed candidate. avg_time_to_place_days is numeric(10,1) which PostgREST serializes as a JSON string, and String.prototype.toFixed does not exist. Empty tenants pass smoke tests; the demo with real data blows up. The same uncoerced row.avg_time_to_place_days.toFixed(1) also lives in source-roi-table.tsx:59 and source-roi-cards.tsx:47, so it takes the buyer-value report down too.
fix: Coerce at the boundary in getSourceAttribution (src/lib/db/source-attribution.ts): map each row to { ...row, total_fee_pence: Number(row.total_fee_pence), avg_time_to_place_days: Number(row.avg_time_to_place_days) } so .toFixed/formatPence/reduce all get real numbers. The buyer-value Source ROI sub-components consume the same data path, so fixing the helper resolves all three render sites.

title: Source-attribution 'Fee revenue' headline is wrong — bigint reduce does string concatenation (e.g. £1,500,002,500.00)
userImpact: totalFeePence = rows.reduce((acc, r) => acc + r.total_fee_pence, 0) seeds a number but total_fee_pence is a bigint-string, so 0 + '150000' concatenates ('0150000250000') and formatPence divides the garbage into a wildly inflated marquee number. The acquirer-facing 'Fee revenue' figure a recruiter points at in the demo is nonsense once 2+ source channels have placements. Per-row figures coerce cleanly so the bug only shows in the summed headline.
fix: Same boundary fix as the crash: coerce total_fee_pence to Number once in getSourceAttribution so the reduce, the topByRevenue sort, and per-row formatPence all do real arithmetic. Do not patch piecemeal at call sites.

title: Buyer-value report: Source ROI sub-table crashes and pipeline/commission money columns are unvalidated bigint strings
userImpact: The 'acquirer due-diligence' report's Source ROI sub-component (source-roi-table.tsx:59 / source-roi-cards.tsx:47) calls row.avg_time_to_place_days.toFixed(1) on a numeric-string and throws, bubbling to the (app) error boundary and replacing the whole page once any source-attributed placement exists. pipeline_value_pence, total_fee_pence and estimated_commission_pence are bigint-strings rendered raw; headline/commission values happen to coerce in division but the sparkline tooltip shows raw strings and nothing is validated. The author only Number()-coerced median_days/p90_days and missed every pence column.
fix: Coerce all bigint/numeric columns to Number inside the buyer-value data helpers (getPipelineValueSparkline → pipeline_value_pence; getCommissionSummary → total_fee_pence, estimated_commission_pence) in src/lib/db/buyer-value.ts, plus the shared getSourceAttribution fix. Map rows once at the helper layer.

title: Buyer-value report (the acquirer-due-diligence dashboard) is unreachable from the Reports hub — no nav entry point
userImpact: The REPORTS array on the hub has only the source-attribution card. /reports/buyer-value is fully built (5 acquirer-due-diligence metric cards, charts, methodology) but has zero inbound links anywhere — top nav, mobile drawer, and hub all point only to /reports. In a live demo the founder navigates to Reports, sees only 'Source attribution', and has no clickable path to the single most sale-relevant report; it looks like it doesn't exist. Graded high (taking the UX lens anchor) because it is the headline 'sale-ready' demo asset.
fix: Add a second ReportCard to the REPORTS array in src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx: { href: '/reports/buyer-value', title: 'Buyer-value report', description: 'Acquirer due-diligence metrics — placements per recruiter, time-to-fill, source ROI, pipeline value, commission.' }. Gate the demo on fixing the buyer-value numeric-string crash above first.

title: Send check-in succeeds with no confirmation and no dashboard refresh — modal just vanishes and the contacted client stays listed
userImpact: On a successful Outlook send the only effect is the dialog closing (onSent → onOpenChange(false)) — no toast, no inline 'Sent' state. This is visually identical to an accidental Escape/outside-click dismissal. The server action calls revalidatePath but the action is invoked programmatically (not as a form-action prop), so without router.refresh() the live page never re-renders: the just-contacted client stays in the Dormant clients widget with stale 'last contact' text and a live 'Send check-in' button. The recruiter cannot tell a real client-facing email sent and may re-send a duplicate. Violates the codebase's own log-note-form/log-activity-form precedent (toast.success + router.refresh).
fix: In send-checkin-modal.tsx, on result.ok show toast.success('Check-in sent to {clientName}') and call router.refresh() (import useRouter) before/after onSent() so the dashboard RSC re-fetches getDormantClients and drops the contacted row. Mirror the existing log-note-form.tsx pattern.

title: Dashboard shows the first-run 'Welcome / add your first candidate' empty state if the count queries error, hiding an established org's real data
userImpact: getDashboardMetrics swallows query errors (Sentry log, then returns count ?? 0). The page computes isEmpty = candidates === 0 && openJobs === 0 and, when true, renders the brand-new-account onboarding screen, fully replacing the dashboard. The two count queries share one connection in a Promise.all, so a correlated blip (network, pooler, RLS/JWT warm-up, statement timeout) collapses both to 0 and an agency with hundreds of candidates is shown 'Welcome to Altus — Add your first candidate' mid-demo, as if all their data vanished. Self-heals on refresh but deeply alarming for a 'your data, instantly searchable' pitch.
fix: Have getDashboardMetrics return an errored flag/discriminant (like the DbResult pattern used by the sibling list helpers) when any count query errors, and only render the first-run EmptyState when the queries SUCCEEDED and returned 0. On error render normal dashboard chrome with an inline 'couldn't load metrics' note. Match src/app/(app)/candidates/page.tsx which already does this.

title: 'Placements this month' metric card is hardcoded to 0 with a 'Lands in Phase 4' caption visible to clients
userImpact: getDashboardMetrics returns placementsThisMonth: 0 unconditionally (stale 'Phase 4' stub) and page.tsx renders it as one of four headline metric cards with caption 'Lands in Phase 4'. The data exists and is computable — the Reports section already aggregates real placement counts from applications where stage='placed'. So the flagship dashboard permanently shows 'Placements this month: 0' next to internal jargon, contradicting the Reports figures and reading to a buyer as broken or as an agency that makes no placements. Placements actually shipped in Phase 3; the comment is wrong.
fix: Compute placementsThisMonth from applications where stage='placed' and coalesce(placed_at, stage_changed_at) within the current month (same source the source-attribution/buyer-value RPCs use), or hide the card entirely until wired up. Remove the client-visible 'Lands in Phase 4' caption either way.

title: Invalid custom date range (from > to or blank) silently snaps back to the 90-day default with no error shown
userImpact: onCustomSubmit validates if (!from || !to || from > to) but the block body is an empty comment — it navigates with the invalid range anyway. The server resolver discards it and returns the default 90-day window. The page then shows last-90-days figures, the preset toggle silently reverts to 'Last 90 days', and the custom form collapses, with no message explaining why the numbers don't match the entered dates. A recruiter who enters a deliberate reversed range may quote the wrong period's placement/revenue figures to a client. Identical empty-validation bug duplicated in buyer-value/date-filter.tsx:72. (Blank fields are mostly caught by the inputs' required attribute; the live UI-reachable failure is from > to.)
fix: On invalid input set local error state and render an inline role='alert' message (e.g. 'To date must be on or after From date') and return early WITHOUT navigating, in both source-attribution/date-filter.tsx and buyer-value/date-filter.tsx. Or swap/clamp the dates before navigating.

title: Source-attribution RPC failure renders £0 / 0 placements totals alongside the error card, looking like real data
userImpact: On RPC failure rows falls back to [] and the page renders an inline error card BUT also unconditionally renders the 'In this window' headline (Placements 0, Fee revenue 0p), the 'By source' table empty state, and the 'Top sources by revenue' empty state — all confidently asserting zero placements/£0 revenue next to the error. A recruiter skimming past the error banner reads it as 'this agency placed nobody', the opposite of the report's purpose. Transient-only path with a visible error banner present, so low, but misleading.
fix: When !result.ok, early-return ONLY the error card and skip the totals/table/top-sources cards (or replace their bodies with an em-dash 'unavailable' placeholder) so a failed load never masquerades as zeroed data.

title: Send check-in error state is a dead end — form unmounts, edited draft lost, no in-place Retry for send failures
userImpact: On a failed Outlook send (Graph 5xx / network), status becomes 'error' but showForm only covers ready|sending|needs_consent, so the subject/body form unmounts and only a bare red error line renders — with no Send/Retry control. The error copy says 'Please try again' but offers no try-again. The only recovery is to close and reopen, which re-runs the full AI draft + up-to-10s poll and overwrites the recruiter's edits with a fresh draft. The sibling 'timeout' state does provide a Retry button, so this is an inconsistency. Uncommon error path, so low.
fix: Keep the form mounted in the 'error' state (add 'error' to showForm, or render the form whenever subject/body are populated) with the error message above it, and add a Retry button that re-calls handleSend without re-drafting.

title: Retry-draft poll loop has no cancellation guard — fires server actions for up to 10s after the modal closes
userImpact: handleRetryDraft duplicates the mount effect's request+poll loop but, unlike go()'s cancelled-flag guard + cleanup, has no cancellation guard. If the recruiter clicks Retry then closes the modal mid-poll, SendCheckinModalBody unmounts but the loop keeps awaiting getLatestOutreachDraftAction (~10s) and calling setStatus/setSubject/setBodyText on the dead instance. On React 19.2.4 there is NO unmounted-setState warning (removed in React 18) and reopening mounts a fresh instance the stale closure cannot touch, so no console spam, no visible flash, no cross-mount corruption — the only residual cost is a few wasted read-only server-action round-trips. Genuine hygiene leak, but the claimed user-facing symptoms do not occur on this stack.
fix: Hoist the cancellation flag to a shared cancelledRef (or AbortController) consulted after every await by both go() and handleRetryDraft, set true in the effect cleanup. Or refactor Retry to bump a retryNonce state the existing effect keys on, so unmount cancellation applies.

title: Reports pages run an unused getProfile fetch that redirects authenticated users to /sign-in on a transient error
userImpact: All three reports pages do const profile = await getProfile(...); if (!profile.ok) redirect('/sign-in') but never reference profile.data for rendering. getProfile collapses all failure modes (including transient 'internal' DB errors) to ok:false, so a momentary blip bounces a logged-in recruiter to the sign-in screen from Reports. It also adds a redundant round-trip. Caveat: the (app) layout already runs the identical getProfile + redirect for every route and actually uses the data, so removing these page-level calls does not eliminate the transient-eject risk — they are redundant dead weight, not a novel failure mode.
fix: Drop the unused getProfile call from the three read-only report pages (auth is already guaranteed by getUser + the (app) layout). If org context is genuinely needed later, redirect only on a true not_found (ok===false AND code==='not_found') and degrade gracefully on internal errors.

title: Dashboard has no loading.tsx — blank/stale screen while 6 parallel queries (recent-activity does ~9 in 3 waves) resolve
userImpact: DashboardPage awaits Promise.all of 6 Supabase round-trips before rendering anything and there is no loading.tsx in the (app) route group (candidates/ has one; the flagship dashboard does not). getRecentActivity alone runs ~9 queries across 3 sequential waves. With no skeleton, a soft nav holds the previous segment and a cold load shows blank, so the landing page feels sluggish on a demo connection. Nothing breaks functionally, hence low (upper end, given it's the demo-critical page).
fix: Add src/app/(app)/loading.tsx rendering skeleton metric cards + widget placeholders. Optionally wrap the slow widgets in <Suspense> so the metric cards paint immediately while activity/dormant-clients stream in.

title: Declined activity rows show the decline reason twice in the Recent Activity feed (human headline + raw enum body)
userImpact: move_application writes decline rows with body = 'Declined — <enum>' (e.g. 'Declined — skills_mismatch') and metadata carrying decline_reason but no 'label' key. entryHeadline falls through to render 'Declined — <human reason>' (e.g. 'Declined — Skills mismatch') and the raw enum body then renders unconditionally below it. So every declined candidate shows the decline twice on adjacent lines in the dashboard feed. Purely cosmetic but visible in the demo feed.
fix: When the headline already encodes the decline reason (kind==='stage_change' with a decline_reason), suppress rendering entry.body for that row in recent-activity-feed.tsx.

title: Completed welcome-checklist steps remain keyboard/screen-reader activatable despite looking disabled
userImpact: Completed steps render a real <Link href=...> disabled only via pointer-events-none + tabIndex={-1} + aria-disabled. pointer-events-none blocks the mouse and tabIndex=-1 removes it from tab order, but the href stays live and aria-disabled is advisory only — a screen-reader user navigating by links rotor, or any programmatic focus + Enter, can still follow a 'done' step (e.g. to /candidates/new). Not data-affecting, bounded (card hides when allDone), but a real a11y correctness gap on the first-run surface.
fix: For done steps render a non-interactive <span>/<div> with matching styling instead of a <Link>, so there is no navigable href for any input modality.

title: Abandoned/retried Send check-in leaves orphan 'email_draft' activities polluting the dashboard Recent Activity feed
userImpact: Each modal open/Retry fires an outreach-draft event and the Inngest function unconditionally INSERTs a new kind='email_draft' activity (no upsert/dedupe). sendOutreachAction only flips the single newest draft to kind='email', so N opens + 1 send leaves N-1 orphan drafts. getRecentActivity SELECTs all kinds with no exclusion, and the feed maps email_draft → 'Drafted an email', so repeatedly opening/backing out fills the 20-row dashboard feed (the first thing in the demo) with 'Drafted an email · {Client}' noise that buries real notes/calls/stage-changes. RLS-scoped, no data loss, diluted over time by real activity — hence low.
fix: Either exclude kind='email_draft' from getRecentActivity's dashboard query (drafts are an internal polling artifact), or have the Inngest function delete/overwrite any prior unsent email_draft for the same company before inserting so at most one draft exists per client.

title: Welcome checklist 'Invite a teammate' step never satisfiable for a solo org and lags real invites
userImpact: The step is marked done only when teamMembers > 1, where teamMembers head-counts public.users rows. A public.users row is created only on invite acceptance (pending invites live in org_invitations), so (a) a genuinely solo operator can never satisfy the step, so allDone never fires and the card never auto-hides (must dismiss manually), and (b) after inviting someone the step still shows not-done until they accept, reading as 'my invite didn't work'. Mitigated by a working Dismiss button and the card only showing on a non-empty org. Cosmetic derived-completion nuance, no data loss.
fix: Base the step's done-state on whether an invitation has been sent (count org_invitations pending invites too), or relabel it as optional so allDone is reachable by a solo user.

title: LinkedIn ingest queries run as the anon role — capture always 500s; feature is dead end-to-end
userImpact: Clicking 'Save to Altus' on a LinkedIn profile always returns a generic 'Ingest failed (500)' / 'profile_not_found'. No candidate is ever created from LinkedIn. The headline AI-first capture feature is completely dead and would fail live if shown in the demo. The route validates the bearer token with supabase.auth.getUser(token) but the cookie-based SSR client never attaches that token to PostgREST, so getProfile and the candidates INSERT run as the Postgres anon role, which RLS denies (current_organization_id() is NULL). Additionally /api/linkedin/ingest is not in PUBLIC_PATHS, so middleware 307-redirects the cookie-less request to /sign-in before the handler even runs.
fix: Build a token-scoped client for the DB queries (createServerClient/createClient with global.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`, or service-role + explicit organization_id scoping) so RLS resolves current_organization_id(). Also add /api/linkedin/ingest to PUBLIC_PATHS (or teach middleware to honor the bearer) so the handler and its OPTIONS preflight actually run. Replace the entirely-it.todo route.test.ts with a real end-to-end test before the demo.

title: Apply form collects salary expectation + availability, validates them, then silently discards both
userImpact: An applicant types a salary expectation (e.g. £55,000) and picks an availability; the form validates and accepts both, shows 'Application received'. But submitApplyAction's createCandidate payload never references parsed.data.salary_expectation or parsed.data.availability — neither is written to the candidate row, the activity metadata, or anywhere. salary_expectation has a real integer column (and the candidate detail page shows 'Expected salary' as blank); availability has no column at all and is lost 100% of the time. On a demo, submitting then opening the empty candidate record exposes the gap. CV parsing can backfill salary only from CV text in some cases, not the typed value.
fix: Add salary_expectation: parsed.data.salary_expectation ? Number(parsed.data.salary_expectation) : null to the createCandidate payload and to CreateCandidateInput. For availability, fold it into the activity metadata / a candidate note (no column exists — ask before migrating). At minimum, remove fields from the form that are thrown away.

title: Apply form hardcodes wrong-brand careers@altus.co.uk in consent text and failure toasts
userImpact: The contact email is hardcoded to careers@altus.co.uk in three public-facing places: the GDPR consent block (page.tsx:46 via renderConsentTextV2) and both CV-upload failure toasts (apply-form.tsx:176, :192). altus.co.uk is neither brand domain (altusrecruit.com web / altusmove.com email) — it is an unrelated, almost-certainly-unmonitored mailbox. On a demo a client spots the wrong brand instantly; an applicant told to email it to fix an upload failure or exercise GDPR rights reaches a dead mailbox (lost lead + compliance liability).
fix: Replace with a real monitored address on the correct brand domain (e.g. careers@altusmove.com) or derive it from the org record / env var so per-org branding doesn't ship a hardcoded wrong-brand GDPR contact tonight.

title: Server 'Invalid CV file' error maps to a non-existent RHF field, so the applicant sees no message
userImpact: When the server rejects the file (e.g. 0-byte but valid-mime PDF, or filename >255 chars — both pass client checks) it returns fieldErrors: { cv: [...] }. The client calls form.setError('cv', ...) but 'cv' is not in applyFormSchema and the file lives in React state, so no <FormMessage> renders it. The spinner stops, the button re-enables, and the applicant sees zero feedback. They retry the same file repeatedly with no idea why, then abandon — lost candidates on a public form.
fix:
Special-case the 'cv' key: route it to the existing setFileError(...) state (which renders a visible role=alert

), only call form.setError for keys that exist in applyFormSchema, and add a fallback toast.error for any fieldError key that isn't a managed RHF field.

title: Email dedup is case-sensitive because /candidates/new doesn't lowercase email
userImpact: candidates.email is plain text with no citext/lower() unique index. The apply form and LinkedIn ingest lowercase email at write time and dedup with .eq on a lowercased term, but the recruiter-facing /candidates/new schema does not lowercase. A recruiter who manually adds 'Alice@Example.com' stores a mixed-case row; a later apply-form/LinkedIn dedup for 'alice@example.com' misses it and creates a duplicate. The same person becomes two candidate records with split history/CVs — silently defeating the dedup the apply/LinkedIn flows were built around.
fix: Add .toLowerCase() to the /candidates/new email schema (and the update path), or normalise in createCandidate plus add a lower(email) functional unique index. Normalise at one consistent boundary to close the hole.

title: Invite RPC failure silently drops the user into a brand-new wrong org; error banner never shown
userImpact: On invite acceptance, exchangeCodeForSession runs (and sets session cookies) before the accept_invitation RPC. If the RPC fails (expired-token race, email mismatch), the user is already authenticated AND has been bootstrapped into a fresh empty org by handle_new_user. The redirect to /sign-in?error=invalid-invite is then itself intercepted by middleware (authed user on /sign-in → strips the error param → bounces to /), so the error banner is never rendered even once. The invited teammate believes they joined the team but lands in an empty org seeing none of the team's data — a silent wrong-tenant outcome.
fix: On invite failure, sign the user out before redirecting to /sign-in?error=..., or render the invite error on the dashboard for authenticated users so the authed bounce can't swallow it.

title: LinkedIn upsert doesn't handle the 23505 it was redesigned around — concurrent capture 500s instead of 'updated'
userImpact: The route's CR-01 comment and the unique-index migration claim the partial unique index converts the concurrent-write race into a 23505 that the upsert helper 'already handles' — but the INSERT error branch has no 23505 detection; it just Sentry-captures and returns code:'internal', which the route maps to HTTP 500 'upsert_failed'. A double-click in the extension (or two recruiters capturing the same profile at once) gives the loser a hard failure toast for an action that actually saved the candidate; the promised 'Updated existing candidate' never fires. First write lands, so no data loss.
fix: In the insert error branch, detect (error as {code?:string}).code === '23505' and recover by re-running the linkedin_url dedup read and switching to the UPDATE path (return created:false), making the CR-01 comment's promise true.

title: Apply re-application dedup is read-then-write with no unique constraint — concurrent submits create duplicate candidates
userImpact: submitApplyAction dedupes by reading getCandidateByEmailForOrg then branching insert/update, with no idempotency key and no DB-level guard (candidates_email_idx is a plain non-unique index; the only partial unique index is linkedin-scoped). Two near-simultaneous submissions from the same email (double-tap, retried request, two tabs) both miss the read and both insert, producing duplicate candidate rows + CVs in the recruiter's pipeline. Recoverable, benign blast radius (single tenant), and unlikely on a controlled demo, but real.
fix: Add a partial unique index on (organization_id, lower(email)) where email is not null (ask before migrating) and map 23505 in createCandidate to re-read + append-CV-to-existing, making the re-application path idempotent at the DB level.

title: confirmApplyAction reports success and navigates even when the CV parse pipeline never queues, with no recruiter-visible signal
userImpact: If inngest.send('cv/uploaded') throws (transient Inngest outage), confirmApplyAction swallows it (Sentry only) and still returns ok — fine for the applicant. But the candidate_cvs row stays parsing_status='pending', the CvReviewPanel shows an indefinite 'Parsing… usually under 30 seconds' spinner with NO retry button (the documented Retry only exists for 'failed'), and no stuck-pending sweep self-heals it. The candidate sits with no extracted profile and a permanent spinner, only a Sentry event — undercutting the 'AI parses every CV automatically' pitch. The embedding cron still gives a thin, degraded embedding, so it isn't fully invisible to search.
fix: Keep returning ok for the applicant, but on send failure flip the row to 'failed' so the existing Retry affordance shows (mirroring the retryParseAction H1 fix that confirmApplyAction was never given), or add a sweep that re-fires cv/uploaded for stuck 'pending' rows.

title: Confirm-stage failure leaves a committed candidate/CV but no parse, and re-submitting mints new orphan CV rows + storage objects
userImpact: If stage-3 confirmApplyAction returns !ok (e.g. Storage list eventual-consistency right after upload), the client just toasts + resets Turnstile and leaves the form populated. Re-submitting re-runs submitApplyAction: the candidate is deduped by email, but nextCVVersion + createCandidateCV unconditionally mint a brand-new pending CV row and a new signed upload URL each retry, and the file is re-PUT. Repeated transient failures accumulate orphaned pending CV versions and duplicate storage objects the recruiter must reconcile. Narrow transient trigger, no data loss.
fix: On confirm failure, either disable further resubmission (tell the applicant the CV is received and to email support) or make submitApplyAction reuse the most recent pending candidate_cvs row instead of always creating a new version on retry.

title: Re-application path doesn't record the freshly-ticked consent (consent_at / consent_text_version stay stale)
userImpact: On the duplicate-email re-application branch the action only bumps market_status cold→actively_looking and logs an activity; it never updates consent_at or consent_text_version, even though the applicant just re-read and re-ticked the current v2 consent. The new-candidate branch stamps them correctly. For a candidate re-applying months later, the audit trail shows a stale consent date/version — weakening the 'demonstrable consent' (UK GDPR Art. 7) posture the project treats as foundational. A contemporaneous activity-log row partially compensates; no demo-visible impact.
fix: In the existing-candidate branch also pass consent_basis:'consent', consent_at:new Date().toISOString(), consent_text_version:CURRENT_CONSENT_VERSION to updateCandidate (extend UpdateCandidateInput to permit the consent_* fields).

title: Re-application path ignores fresh contact-detail updates (phone/location/role) from the new submission
userImpact: When an existing candidate re-applies, only market_status is bumped and an activity logged; freshly-submitted full_name/phone/location/current_role_title are not merged. The applicant thinks they updated their profile; they didn't. Real residual gap is narrow: the re-applied CV is parsed and fill-empty-merges blank fields, so only a field that was blank AND not extracted from the new CV stays stale. A populated-but-changed phone would be left stale by both current code and a fill-empty fix by design (don't clobber recruiter edits), so the 'recruiter calls the old number' framing is overstated.
fix: On the re-application branch, fill-empty-only update of phone/location/current_role_title/full_name (mirror the LinkedIn upsert fill-empty pattern) and record what changed in the activity metadata.

title: Apply success page 'our website' link (and PWA start_url) point to /, redirecting unauthenticated candidates to recruiter sign-in
userImpact: After a successful application the success page links 'our website' to /, which is the authenticated dashboard and is not in PUBLIC_PATHS, so middleware redirects the unauthenticated candidate to /sign-in?next=/ — the recruiter's internal magic-link sign-in form they can't use. The PWA manifest start_url:'/' has the same problem. Occurs only after the application already succeeded (no funnel/data loss; 'close this window' copy still works), so it's a trust/perception mislink — but exactly the surface a client pokes during a demo of the public funnel.
fix: Point the link at the org's public marketing site or an external URL (e.g. https://altusrecruit.com), or remove it and keep only the 'you can close this window' copy. Fix manifest start_url similarly.

title: Sign-up accepts whitespace-only org/full name, bootstrapping a blank-named org via trigger
userImpact: SignUpForm passes organization_name/full_name straight into signInWithOtp metadata with no trim/min-length check, and the form is rendered noValidate so even the HTML required never fires. handle_new_user uses nullif(value,'') which only nulls a truly empty string, so a single-space org name survives and renders as a blank org name in TopNav and anywhere org.name shows. Requires deliberately typing only spaces; it is self-recoverable via Settings → Organization (so 'no way to fix it' is overstated).
fix: Trim and enforce min length (e.g. 2 chars) on organizationName and fullName before signInWithOtp (mirror the apply form's z.string().trim().min(2)), and/or add a not-blank guard in the trigger.

title: Apply-form rate limiter fails open on every error and its select-then-update increment races
userImpact: checkApplyFormRateLimit returns allowed:true on select error, update error, and any thrown exception, and the select→update increment is non-atomic, so a burst can read the same count and all pass. The documented '3 per 5 min' cap is best-effort and bypassable under load or a transient DB error. Real-world impact is bounded: Turnstile runs before the limiter and fails CLOSED when TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY is unset, so there is no production config where this limiter is the sole gate. Defence-in-depth abuse-surface hardening, not a present-tense exploit.
fix: Move the increment into a single SECURITY DEFINER plpgsql function doing an atomic UPSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET count = count + 1 RETURNING count and deny over the cap; on a public endpoint prefer fail-closed-with-retry over unlimited writes; make TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY mandatory in production.

title: LinkedIn ingest CORS allows any chrome-extension origin when LINKEDIN_EXTENSION_ID is unset, with Allow-Credentials: true
userImpact: When LINKEDIN_EXTENSION_ID is unset (optional in env, no production guard), corsHeadersFor reflects ANY chrome-extension://[a-p]{32} origin and sets access-control-allow-credentials:true, so a forgotten env var in production silently widens CORS to every Chrome extension. Practical impact is minimal: the real gate is the bearer token (which a foreign extension can't obtain), and allow-credentials is moot since the endpoint never authenticates via cookies. Defence-in-depth config hygiene; no path to cross-tenant data.
fix: Make LINKEDIN_EXTENSION_ID required in production (superRefine when NODE_ENV==='production') and drop the regex fallback outside development; in production echo Allow-Origin only for the exact pinned id.

title: LinkedIn dedup SELECTs omit organization_id filter (and the source='linkedin' predicate), relying on RLS the caller doesn't satisfy
userImpact: getCandidateByLinkedInUrl / getCandidateByEmailLowercase query candidates with no .eq('organization_id', ...) (and the URL lookup also omits source='linkedin', so its scope disagrees with the partial unique index), trusting a 'route is authenticated' premise that is false for the anon-running ingest route. Today the feature just 500s. The latent risk: if someone 'fixes' the 500 by switching to the service-role client, these unfiltered getters would scan all orgs and .limit(1) could dedup against a foreign tenant's row — a cross-tenant read. The upsert's existing org-mismatch fail-closed guard blocks a foreign-row WRITE, and the getters have no other callers today, so it's latent fragility, not a present leak.
fix: Add an explicit organizationId param to both getters and append .eq('organization_id', organizationId); add .eq('source', 'linkedin') to the URL lookup so it matches exactly the rows the unique index governs. Makes the helpers safe-by-construction regardless of RLS vs service-role.

title: Jobs created from an approved spec call are never embedded or match-scored (wrong Inngest event name 'jobs/jd-changed')
userImpact: The headline 'spec call -> approve JD -> instantly matched candidates' demo flow silently half-works. A spec-created job is invisible to semantic search for up to ~10 minutes (only picked up by the NULL-embedding cron sweep) and NEVER gets the precomputed Sonnet match explanations that manually-created jobs get, because the score chain is only emitted by embed-job-on-jd-change, which never fires. The code comment claiming 'embed-job-on-jd-change picks it up' is factually wrong.
fix: Change the event name in create-job-from-spec.ts:216 from 'jobs/jd-changed' to 'job/embed' to match embedJobOnJDChange's trigger and the two manual job-create server actions (jobs/new/actions.ts, clients/[id]/jobs/new/actions.ts) that already use 'job/embed'. One-line fix restores the embed + match-score chain.

title: Outlook subscription recreate path reports SUCCESS even when the new subscription_id DB write fails (stale credentials, email sync silently stops)
userImpact: After a Microsoft subscription expires and is recreated, if the DB write of the new subscription_id/client_state silently fails (updateOutlookSubscriptionState and setOutlookDeltaLink return non-throwing DbResults that are ignored), the credentials row keeps the stale/expired subscription_id while the dashboard/renewal log show 'recreated: success'. The webhook can no longer match the new id, so inbound email->activity sync silently stops for that recruiter while the system claims it's healthy. Unlike the inline renew path (line 207), which correctly checks writeResult.ok.
fix: Inside tryRecreate(), capture the DbResult of updateOutlookSubscriptionState (line 313) and setOutlookDeltaLink (line 321); if either is ok:false, throw so the caller's catch records recordRenewalAttempt(success:false) and increments failed++ instead of recreated++. Mirror the existing inline-renew guard.

title: POST /api/linkedin/ingest has no rate limiting — an authenticated recruiter token can drive unbounded billable Voyage embeds and flood the candidate list
userImpact: A single leaked/abused recruiter Bearer token (or a buggy extension retry loop) can POST in a tight loop; each call upserts a candidate row and fires a billable Voyage embed (varying linkedin_url forces a fresh INSERT + embed every time). There is no per-user/per-org throttle and no per-window cap — unlike the public apply form, which enforces a 3/5min/IP/org Postgres sliding window. Uncapped AI cost amplification plus junk-row creation reachable from a normal user session, undermining the per-tenant cost visibility the product sells on.
fix: Add a per-user (or per-org) sliding-window throttle before the upsert/embed dispatch, reusing the apply-form Postgres rate-limit pattern (src/lib/integrations/apply-form-rate-limit.ts). Return 429 + Retry-After when exceeded to bound both AI spend and junk-row creation.

title: Sentry beforeSend scrubs extra/contexts by key allowlist but never inspects exception messages — raw Postgres/PostgREST errors echoing row values flow to Sentry unscrubbed
userImpact: The global beforeSend deletes cookies, strips user.email, and runs a key-allowlist scrub over event.extra/contexts only — it never touches event.message or event.exception.values[].value. The dominant pattern across src/lib/db/** passes the RAW Postgres/PostgREST error to captureException, and a constraint-violation message echoes the conflicting row value (e.g. 'Key (email)=(jane@example.com) already exists'). That candidate PII lands in the unscrubbed exception value, violating the hard 'never log PII' rule, with no automated backstop. Reaches only the agency's own Sentry project (not cross-tenant, not demo-visible), but is a real compliance/audit-posture gap.
fix: Add a defence-in-depth pass in beforeSend (and the identical sentry.edge.config.ts) over event.message and each event.exception.values[].value — redact strings above a length threshold (CV/transcript text is long) and apply an email-address regex scrub. Keep the call-site wrapping convention but stop relying on it as the only barrier.

title: ai_usage cost-log RPC failure is swallowed on every AI wrapper — calls succeed while cost is silently dropped from the per-tenant ledger
userImpact: runWithLogging (claude.ts), embed (voyage.ts) and transcribe (whisper.ts) wrap record_ai_usage in try/catch that captures to Sentry but returns the AI result anyway. If the RPC flakes (transient service-role/Postgres blip, or a misconfigured-but-nonempty SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY that passes z.string().min(1) boot validation), every AI call still succeeds while its cost is dropped from ai_usage — the source of truth CLAUDE.md calls non-negotiable. /settings/usage then under-reports real spend, so the founder's SaaS pricing/margin decisions rest on incomplete data. Not demo-visible; the only current signal is a stream of default-level Sentry exceptions a non-technical owner won't read.
fix: Keep non-fatal, but (a) add a single retry of the log write before giving up, and (b) capture as a Sentry message with level:'error' and a distinct tag (e.g. ai_usage_drop) so an alert can page on sustained failure. Optionally emit an Inngest event to a dead-letter table so dropped cost rows can be reconciled.

title: CV parse marks parsing_status 'complete' even when the auto-fill candidate-field merge write fails
userImpact: In the 'write-extracted' step, markCandidateFieldsFromCV() (the whole point of the D-08 auto-fill) is awaited with its DbResult discarded — and it returns ok:false rather than throwing on a DB error. So a failed candidate UPDATE is swallowed and the CV row is still committed 'complete'. A recruiter sees 'Parsed ✓' but the candidate's empty fields (email, phone, skills, work history) were never populated, with no signal and no retry. The manual path (acceptCVFieldsAction) correctly checks this result; the Inngest path omits the guard.
fix: Capture the result of markCandidateFieldsFromCV; on !result.ok, throw inside the step so Inngest retries (the merge is idempotent — fills empties only) rather than committing 'complete' with an un-enriched candidate.

title: Cost-log catch blocks in claude.ts and voyage.ts pass the raw SDK/RPC error to Sentry instead of wrapping to name-only (PII policy violation)
userImpact: The record_ai_usage catch blocks in claude.ts (line 93) and voyage.ts (line 138) call Sentry.captureException(logErr) with the raw error, violating the explicit in-repo VERIFICATION R4 policy that whisper.ts and every Inngest function follow ('never pass the raw error; wrap to name only'). These are the two wrappers that send actual CV/candidate prompts to the model. In practice the caught error here is the subsequent Postgres RPC error (the model call already succeeded), so the realistic PII-leak probability is lower than a model-SDK error — but it is a genuine isolated violation of a documented policy and inconsistent with every sibling file. Not user-visible.
fix: Mirror whisper.ts / use formatErrorForSentry: wrap to name only — const name = logErr instanceof Error ? logErr.name : 'UnknownError'; Sentry.captureException(new Error('record_ai_usage:' + name), {...}) — in both claude.ts and voyage.ts.

title: Whisper clamps durationSeconds to a minimum of 1, making the 'unreadable audio' guard in the spec pipeline dead code (and stale docstring)
userImpact: transcribe() does Math.max(1, Math.round(verbose.duration ?? 0)), so it can never return <=0 even when Whisper reports no duration — contradicting its own docstring (claims 'durationSeconds <= 0 -> throws'). The downstream guard in transcribe-and-structure-spec.ts:196 (durationSeconds <= 0 -> return -1 -> 'Could not read the audio file') is therefore unreachable dead code. Real user impact is minimal in practice: corrupt files are blocked upstream by ffmpeg, and silent/empty transcripts are caught by the line-230 empty-text guard, so no garbage JD draft reaches 'ready_for_review'. The residual cost is sub-penny over-billing and a slightly less-apt (but still clean) failure message in a near-impossible case.
fix: In whisper.ts, separate the cost clamp from the validity signal: if verbose.duration is missing or <=0, throw (matching the docstring) or return durationSeconds:0 so the caller's guard fires. Use Math.max(1, ...) only when computing cost, not for the returned duration.

title: precompute-matches scored/cache_hits counters are mutated inside step.run callbacks and report 0 after Inngest memoized replays
userImpact: cacheHits++ / scored++ run inside per-candidate step.run callbacks, but Inngest re-executes the outer generator on each step boundary and restores memoized step results without re-running the callback. So on the final pass the closure counters stay at 0, and the function returns scored:0, cache_hits:0 even when 10 candidates were scored. No data corruption — match summaries are still written by upsertMatchSummary inside the steps — but the return payload (visible only in the Inngest dashboard) misreports work, which will mislead the founder debugging 'did matching run?'.
fix: Have each step.run('score-...') RETURN a small result ({ outcome: 'scored' | 'cache_hit' | 'skipped' }) and accumulate counts from the awaited memoized return values in the outer scope, mirroring embed-batch.ts's correct pattern.

title: Voyage embed() maps response.data to vectors by array position with no length/order validation; silent skips and a latent reorder-misalignment risk
userImpact: embed() builds vectors by positional .map() with no assertion that response.data.length === inputs.length and discards the SDK's per-item index field. In embed-batch.ts the callers zip vectors[i] with usable[i] and bare-continue on an empty vector, with no Sentry breadcrumb. Best case (current Voyage behaviour): a row stays NULL-embedded and is self-healed by the next 10-min NULL sweep — only a delay plus zero observability. Worst case (latent, requires undocumented reorder/partial return): a candidate is assigned the wrong person's vector, never re-queued, and 'senior Python dev' returns the wrong human — the exact failure AI search is meant to prevent. Not an active failure under today's provider behaviour.
fix: In embed(): assert response.data.length === args.inputs.length (throw/Sentry-warn on mismatch) and re-sort by the SDK's d.index before mapping. In embed-batch.ts callers, emit a Sentry breadcrumb instead of a bare continue when a vector is empty/missing so NULL-embedding stalls are observable.

title: Sonnet pricing constants duplicated across claude.ts, ad-generate.ts, and jd-extract.ts — future drift will desync denormalised cost_pence columns from the ai_usage ledger
userImpact: Canonical Sonnet pricing (240 input / 1200 output pence per MTok) lives in claude.ts PRICING_PENCE_PER_MTOK (the value written to the authoritative ai_usage ledger). ad-generate.ts re-declares 240/1200 for job_ads.cost_pence and jd-extract.ts re-derives it inline for spec_drafts.sonnet_cost_pence, guarded only by a 'sync here too' comment and no equality test. All three agree today (no present bug), but the comments say to re-verify pricing before next launch; updating claude.ts alone would silently leave the two denormalised display columns wrong while ai_usage stays correct, eroding trust in the per-tenant cost numbers the owner uses for margin decisions.
fix: Export a calcCostPence(model, inputTokens, outputTokens) helper (and the pricing table) from claude.ts and import it in ad-generate.ts and jd-extract.ts so there is a single source of truth for the formula and rounding semantics. At minimum add a unit test asserting the three constants match.

title: Ad generation and inclusivity-scoring Sonnet calls have no per-org monthly spend ceiling (only match_score is capped)
userImpact: env.MAX_MONTHLY_MATCH_SPEND_PENCE is enforced in the match-scoring paths (precompute + explainCandidateMatchAction), but getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth only sums purpose='match_score'. generateAdAction (~1.8p) and scoreInclusivityAction (~0.7p) — plus spec_jd_extract, dormant_outreach_draft, cv_parse — call Sonnet with no spend guard. A recruiter (or scripted/runaway use) repeatedly clicking 'Generate ad' or scoring ads can burn unbounded Sonnet cost with no product-side brake. A visibility backstop exists (every call writes ai_usage, /settings/usage surfaces per-tenant spend) and per-call cost is small, so this is a hardening/consistency gap, not a demo-breaking or data-integrity bug.
fix: Generalise the spend ceiling: make getOrgMatchSpendThisMonth (or a sibling) sum all Sonnet purposes for the org/month, and apply a ceiling check in generateAdAction, scoreInclusivityAction, and the spec/outreach Inngest functions, with a Sentry warning so operators have a brake.

title: Browser Supabase client uses default navigator.locks (no noopLock), deviating from the documented anti-wedge convention
userImpact: createBrowserClient is called with no auth options, so it uses the default navigator.locks token lock — a deviation from the global CLAUDE.md convention to pass auth:{ lock: noopLock } for single-account SPAs. The claimed sign-in/sign-out 'wedge' impact is overstated for this codebase: installed @supabase/ssr is 0.10.3 (the documented lesson scopes the bug to <=0.10.2), there is no verifyOtp call and no client-side onAuthStateChange/AuthProvider subscriber, and the client is instantiated fresh per click — so the canonical parallel-init race that produces the wedge cannot form. Cheap convention-alignment hardening, not a substantiated live risk.
fix: Add the documented noopLock (const noopLock = async (_n, _t, fn) => fn()) and pass { auth: { lock: noopLock } } to createBrowserClient in src/lib/supabase/client.ts, per global CLAUDE.md guidance.

title: LinkedIn embed purpose 'linkedin_candidate_embed' is forced through the EmbedPurpose union with `as never`, defeating the typo guard
userImpact: voyage.ts's EmbedPurpose is a closed union ('candidate_embed' | 'job_embed' | 'search_query_embed') and the LinkedIn function passes purpose:'linkedin_candidate_embed' as never to slip an off-union literal past the compiler. Cost IS still logged (ai_usage.purpose is free text), so no current user impact and the /settings/usage breakdown renders correctly today. The risk is purely a future silent mis-categorisation of LinkedIn embed spend if the literal is ever mistyped, since the compiler is told to ignore it.
fix: Add 'linkedin_candidate_embed' to the EmbedPurpose union in voyage.ts and drop the `as never` cast so the purpose string is compiler-checked like every other one.

title: Inngest client module (holding INNGEST_EVENT_KEY) lacks `import 'server-only'`, unlike every sibling secret-bearing infra module
userImpact: src/lib/inngest/client.ts constructs new Inngest({ eventKey: env.INNGEST_EVENT_KEY }) (a server secret) but omits the `import 'server-only'` first line that service.ts, encryption.ts, and every src/lib/ai/*.ts wrapper use. No current user-facing impact — all 28 importers are server actions/route handlers/Inngest functions, no client component imports it, and t3-env's server/client split would throw as a secondary guard. It is an inconsistent guard on a secret-holding module, weakening the 'server-only modules cannot reach the client bundle' invariant.
fix: Add `import 'server-only'` as the first line of src/lib/inngest/client.ts to match service.ts and the AI wrappers, so an accidental client-side import becomes a compile-time error rather than relying on t3-env's runtime throw.

title: Outlook OAuth state cookie cleared without secure:true while set with __Host- prefix — deletion may silently no-op, leaving a stale CSRF state cookie
userImpact: The CSRF state cookie '__Host-outlook-oauth-state' is cleared at four sites in the callback with { path:'/', maxAge:0 } but no secure:true. Per the __Host- prefix rule, a browser rejects a Set-Cookie (including a Max-Age=0 deletion) that lacks Secure, so the defensive clear silently no-ops. Impact is minimal: the CSRF guard itself is an unaffected strict equality check, the cookie is HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Lax (not JS-readable), the 600s maxAge expires it within 10 minutes, and every new OAuth attempt overwrites it via a valid __Host- write — so stale state cannot accumulate or be replayed. Hygiene/defence-in-depth gap, not exploitable.
fix: Add secure:true (keep path:'/') to every res.cookies.set(STATE_COOKIE, ...) clear in src/app/api/outlook/callback/route.ts (lines 78, 184, 210, 221) so the __Host- prefixed deletion is accepted by the browser.
